DASHBOARD: SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION

PermID5000317077Websitehttp://www.suffolk.com/IndustryConstruction & Engineering

ACTIVITIES:

Suffolk Construction, founded in 1982 by John Fish in Boston, Massachusetts, is a leading U.S. general contractor. With $5.5 billion in revenue and 2,500 employees, Suffolk delivers complex projects in healthcare, education, and commercial sectors. Notable works include Bostons Millennium Tower and Miamis Brickell City Centre. Employing advanced technologies like BIM and virtual design, Suffolk enhances project efficiency and safety. The company operates 10 offices nationwide, with a strong Northeast presence. Suffolks commitment to sustainability includes LEED-certified projects and eco-friendly practices. Its employee-centric culture, with robust training and diversity initiatives, earned it a spot on Fortunes Best Workplaces list. Suffolk continues to innovate, focusing on smart buildings and urban development.

+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION'S LANE REOPENING ON ROUTE 58 SIGNALS PROGRESS

SUFFOLK, Va. — Motorists on U.S. Route 58 near downtown Suffolk will soon see relief as the Virginia Department of Transportation (VDOT) begins removing a long-term lane closure starting as early as Monday night. As of 5th May 2026, the right lane closure on Route 58 westbound just east of the downtown exit will be reopened, allowing all three lanes of westbound traffic to flow freely once again.The lane closure has been in effect since March, coinciding with the ongoing Southeastern Public Service Authority interchange project managed by Suffolk Construction, which began in August 2024. The completion of this project aims to facilitate a new ramp and flyover for eastbound traffic, enabling vehicles to access the regional landfill without crossing over westbound lanes, thereby improving traffic safety and efficiency.While the reopening of the lane is a welcome change, motorists should be aware that some temporary overnight closures may occur as crews work to remove the temporary barriers. These closures will take place from 8 p.m. to 5 a.m., depending on weather conditions and scheduling factors. Long-term shoulder closures remain in effect in both directions, and intermittent lane closures or brief stoppages may still be possible as the project progresses.The speed limit through this construction zone is set at 50 miles per hour, and speed ticket cameras are actively monitoring both directions to ensure compliance. Motorists are encouraged to stay informed about the ongoing work and any potential disruptions by visiting VDOT's dedicated project website.As Suffolk Construction continues to make strides on this critical infrastructure project, the reopening of Route 58 is a significant milestone that reflects the company's commitment to enhancing transportation in the region. Further updates regarding the project will be available as work continues.

+ VMO2 SECURES 10-YEAR SOLAR ENERGY PACT WITH SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION

Virgin Media O2 (VMO2) is taking a significant step towards enhancing its renewable energy footprint by entering into a 10-year Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with solar power provider Egg Power. This strategic collaboration will allow VMO2 to source energy from Egg's 70MW solar farm located in Suffolk, which is under construction and anticipated to commence power generation in 2027.The agreement, announced on 1st May 2026, is set to supply approximately 5% of VMO2's total energy demands, marking a noteworthy addition to the company's sustainability initiatives. Egg Power, as a natural energy partner for VMO2, is particularly aligned with the company’s goals, given that both entities are under the ownership of Liberty Global. This partnership is expected to significantly aid VMO2 in its pursuit of achieving Net Zero carbon emissions by 2040.Mark Hardman, Director of Finance Operations at VMO2, emphasized the importance of this agreement, stating, “This agreement with Egg Power is the latest step in Virgin Media O2’s journey to achieve net zero emissions by the end of 2040. We’re committed to growing and operating our business in a way that’s good for people and the planet, where we’re cutting carbon, securing renewable energy on a long-term basis, and sourcing renewable energy generation from the UK.”This new PPA with Egg Power complements a previous 10-year agreement VMO2 entered into for wind power with The Renewables Infrastructure Group last year. Together, these two agreements will enable VMO2 to source around 20% of its energy from renewable sources through Power Purchase Agreements, reinforcing the company's commitment to sustainability.As VMO2 continues to advance its renewable energy strategy, this latest partnership not only addresses immediate energy needs but also aligns with broader environmental goals. The growing reliance on renewable energy sources is becoming increasingly critical for companies like VMO2 as they navigate the challenges of climate change and strive for sustainable operational practices.

+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION BRACES FOR $897M CAPITAL SPENDING PROPOSAL

Suffolk County Executive Edward P. Romaine has unveiled a capital budget proposal totaling $897 million for 2027, which aims to address critical infrastructure needs within Suffolk County. The proposal, revealed on 24th April 2026, is part of a broader three-year capital program that includes approximately $3 billion in investments, maintaining a similar figure to a previously adopted plan.This proposed budget marks a 15% increase compared to the adopted 2026 capital budget, emphasizing long-term public works projects. Romaine highlighted that the budget focuses on "infrastructure needs that have been neglected for so long," framing it as an investment in Suffolk County's future. The capital budget operates independently from the operating budget, which is anticipated in the fall.Among the key components of the 2027 budget are $208 million allocated for sewer upgrades, $200 million dedicated to a new transportation hub near the Ronkonkoma Long Island Rail Road station, and $128 million for various road, bridge, sidewalk, and public transit improvements. A significant aspect of the proposal is the $185 million earmarked for a new medical examiner building, which will feature advanced ventilation, security, and lab facilities. The current building, dating back to the late 1980s, has been deemed beyond repair.Construction on the new medical examiner facility is set to commence in 2027 following a $20 million bond authorized in February 2025 for design and engineering costs. The project is projected to be completed by 2029. Additionally, the budget outlines plans for the new Smith Point Bridge, which will enhance connectivity between Shirley and Fire Island, funded primarily through federal aid.In introducing the budget, Romaine, a Republican in his third year as County Executive, stressed its role in supporting farmland preservation, expanding recreational assets, and promoting growth in key economic areas. Presiding Officer Anthony Piccirillo has indicated that a working group will be established to review the proposed budget, with meetings scheduled to start in May following an analysis by the legislature's budget review office.

+ BALFOUR BEATTY [3 News That Matters]

+ BALFOUR BEATTY ELEVATES TRENT JOHNSON TO LEAD AVIATION OPERATIONS IN THE CAROLINAS

Balfour Beatty has promoted Trent Johnson to Vice President of operations in the Carolinas, effective immediately. Johnson's new role will see him leading and expanding the company’s aviation sector across North and South Carolina, building on a career that spans over 19 years in the construction industry.As of 1st April 2026, Trent Johnson’s promotion is a reflection of his extensive experience, including more than 12 and a half years with Balfour Beatty. During his tenure, he has successfully contributed to the delivery of 22 aviation projects, ranging from targeted renovations to complex, multi-phase programmes. His leadership has been pivotal in enhancing Balfour Beatty’s aviation presence, notably at Raleigh-Durham International Airport (RDU) and Albert J. Ellis Airport.In his new capacity, Johnson will continue to support Balfour Beatty’s existing aviation clients while expanding the company’s footprint at additional regional and international airports throughout the Carolinas. His focus will remain on assisting project teams in navigating the complexities of active airport environments, while prioritizing safety, operational continuity, and stakeholder coordination.Jeff Sandeen, President of Balfour Beatty in the Carolinas, commented on Johnson’s promotion, stating, “Trent has built a strong reputation as a trusted leader and client partner within our local aviation operations. His ability to guide teams through the complexities of active airport environments has been a key driver of our success in the region.” Sandeen expressed confidence that Johnson will play a crucial role in shaping the next phase of growth for Balfour Beatty’s aviation portfolio.Trent Johnson holds a degree in Construction Engineering Management from North Carolina State University and is also known for his community involvement, particularly with the Make-A-Wish Foundation. His steady leadership style and collaborative approach have helped Balfour Beatty deliver projects that align with long-term regional growth and infrastructure priorities.

+ MAINMARK UK JOINS BALFOUR BEATTY'S CP7 FRAMEWORK FOR RAIL INFRASTRUCTURE

Ground engineering specialist Mainmark UK has secured a place on the Balfour Beatty plc CP7 Framework, positioning the company to support the delivery of key rail infrastructure works across Network Rail’s latest five-year investment programme. This investment period, known as Control Period 7 (CP7), spans from 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2029, with an allocation of approximately £43–45 billion across Great Britain.The CP7 programme focuses on maintaining, renewing, and modernising the rail network to deliver a safer, more reliable, and more sustainable railway. Investment priorities during CP7 include infrastructure maintenance and renewals, digital signalling and technology, safety improvements, and initiatives aimed at enhancing both freight and passenger performance across the network.As part of the CP7 Framework, contractors will engage in a variety of works aligned with Network Rail’s Strategic Business Plan. These activities will encompass low-complexity emergency callouts, planned preventative maintenance, and small-scale civil engineering schemes. Mainmark UK will be involved in geotechnical response works, bridge repairs, structural integrity projects, platform refurbishments, and drainage clearance, among others.Tom Kavanagh, General Manager at Mainmark UK, expressed enthusiasm about the collaboration with Balfour Beatty, stating, “We’re looking forward to collaborating with Balfour Beatty to help deliver critical infrastructure works across the network during the CP7 period. Being selected to support Balfour Beatty on this framework is a fantastic opportunity for our team and aligns perfectly with our growth plans.”Mainmark is known for its innovative ground engineering solutions, particularly its Teretek® resin injection technology, which stabilises weak ground conditions and fills subsurface voids without extensive excavation. This technology is crucial for maintaining the integrity of rail infrastructure, especially as heavier freight loads and severe weather events put increasing pressure on railway assets. With its appointment to the CP7 Framework, Mainmark UK is set to play a vital role in ensuring the resilience and reliability of the UK's rail network.

+ BALFOUR BEATTY'S GROWTH FUELED BY RECORD ORDER BOOK

Balfour Beatty PLC has reported impressive growth, driven by its robust performance in the construction sector, particularly in power transmission projects. The company’s market valuation has soared nearly two-thirds over the past year, a noteworthy achievement for a FTSE 250 constituent. This growth trajectory is largely attributed to a record order book and a 9 percent increase in underlying earnings per share, which reached 47.6 pence.On 11th March 2026, the construction group revealed that its top-line growth was significantly supported by the increasing demand for power transmission in the UK and a surge in building projects in the US. Operating profits within Balfour Beatty's support services segment saw a remarkable increase of one-third, amounting to £122 million, alongside an 80 basis point rise in the underlying margin to 8.5 percent. The company’s UK construction division achieved its margin targets ahead of schedule, underscoring its operational efficiency.In the US market, Balfour Beatty has capitalized on rising building volumes although it faced challenges due to cost overruns in a project within its Civils arm. The dual market strategy appears to be paying off, as the company navigates through these hurdles while maintaining a focus on profitability. The expansion of infrastructure projects, particularly in power transmission, has provided a solid foundation for growth, enabling Balfour Beatty to solidify its position in the competitive construction landscape.Market analysts remain optimistic about Balfour Beatty’s future prospects, considering its substantial order book which continues to grow amidst a backdrop of increasing infrastructure investment. The company’s strategic initiatives to enhance operational margins and deliver projects on time have positioned it well for sustained growth in the coming quarters. Investors are likely to monitor Balfour Beatty’s performance closely as it seeks to leverage its strengths in both the UK and US markets.As Balfour Beatty continues to capitalize on the opportunities presented by the expanding power grid and overall construction demand, it remains crucial for stakeholders to assess the potential impacts of rising costs and project management challenges. Overall, the company’s recent performance indicates a strong recovery trajectory, making it a noteworthy player in the construction sector as it heads into the next fiscal year.

+ HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO [3 News That Matters]

+ TRACK3D SECURES MAJOR PARTNERSHIP WITH HENSEL PHELPS FOR CONSTRUCTION TECHNOLOGY

Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, has marked a significant milestone by securing a multi-year agreement with Hensel Phelps, expanding its collaboration from a single pilot to the deployment of Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform across more than 200 active projects. This announcement was made on 1st April 2026, emphasizing Track3D's growing influence in the construction sector.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize the use of Track3D for portfolio-wide progress monitoring, indicating strong confidence in the platform’s scalability and effectiveness across diverse construction projects. By utilizing automated progress tracking, Hensel Phelps aims to identify on-site issues earlier, which can significantly reduce delays and enhance schedule reliability, especially in complex builds.A notable case study from San Francisco International Airport highlighted the successful application of Track3D’s technology on a challenging infrastructure project. Project leaders reported a shift from reactive issue resolution to proactive decision-making, facilitated by 360deg site walks and the involvement of existing field personnel, thereby minimizing the need for major workflow changes. This deployment at SFO reinforces Track3D's relevance in high-value infrastructure work and solidifies its position in the reality intelligence market.In addition to the partnership with Hensel Phelps, Track3D has introduced a March update to its platform that focuses on large-enterprise oversight and workflow efficiency. Key enhancements include an admin dashboard and Groups functionality, which are designed to provide major contractors with centralized portfolio visibility and streamlined user management. Furthermore, the new capture-free progress tracking feature allows teams to document job site status directly on floorplans, promoting continuous oversight.Track3D is also intensifying its go-to-market strategy by targeting specialty contractors, with plans to participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026. The company aims to showcase tools that automate production tracking and reduce manual reporting for drywall and ceiling professionals. Collectively, these developments—ranging from the Hensel Phelps portfolio rollout to enterprise-focused product enhancements—underscore Track3D’s strengthening competitive position in construction technology and may pave the way for sustainable growth in the future.

+ MICAH CHOY STEPS UP AS PROJECT SUPERINTENDENT AT HENSEL PHELPS

Micah (Pono) Choy has been promoted to project superintendent at Hensel Phelps, overseeing the Hawaii Department of Education’s Regional Kitchen – North Central Oahu project in Wahiawa. As of 1st April 2026, Choy's promotion marks a significant milestone in his career with the company, which he joined in 2018.During his tenure at Hensel Phelps, Choy has played a pivotal role in various major projects across the aviation, wastewater, and federal market sectors in Hawaii and Guam. His collaborative leadership style has garnered recognition, allowing him to build strong partnerships across teams and effectively drive project outcomes.Choy's promotion to project superintendent reflects Hensel Phelps' commitment to developing talent within the organization. His experience and expertise will be instrumental in managing the Regional Kitchen project, which is critical for enhancing educational facilities in Hawaii.The construction industry continues to evolve, and Hensel Phelps is positioning itself for future growth by promoting leaders like Choy. His ability to navigate complex projects while fostering teamwork aligns with the company's strategic goals.As Hensel Phelps embarks on new initiatives, Choy's leadership will likely contribute to the successful execution of high-profile projects. Stakeholders and investors will be watching closely as the company continues to build its reputation in the construction sector.

+ TRACK3D PARTNERS WITH HENSEL PHELPS FOR MAJOR CONSTRUCTION ROLLOUT

Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, has announced a significant milestone in its growth trajectory as it secures a multi-year enterprise agreement with Hensel Phelps. As of 25th March 2026, this collaboration expands from a single pilot project to deployment across more than 200 active projects, marking a notable shift in how the construction industry embraces advanced technology.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform portfolio-wide for progress monitoring, demonstrating a strong confidence in the technology's scalability across diverse project types. This extensive rollout is expected to deepen recurring revenue streams for Track3D, enhance customer loyalty, and establish a high-profile reference account that could attract additional large contractor deals.In addition to the enterprise agreement, Track3D highlighted a successful case study at San Francisco International Airport. Hensel Phelps utilized Track3D's technology on this demanding build, where project leaders noted that the platform facilitated a shift from reactive to proactive decision-making. The deployment at SFO showcases how Track3D can enhance progress tracking and coordination across trades without necessitating significant changes to existing workflows.On the product development front, Track3D has announced a March release aimed at improving enterprise oversight and workflow efficiency. New features include an admin dashboard and Groups functionality, which allow for centralized portfolio visibility and streamlined user management. Furthermore, enhancements such as capture-free progress tracking capabilities enable teams to document job site status directly on floorplans, marking a significant improvement in operational flexibility for enterprise users.Track3D is also advancing its go-to-market strategy by focusing on specialty contractors, with plans to participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026. This event is pivotal for drywall and ceiling professionals, and Track3D aims to showcase tools that automate production tracking and enhance margin protection. Overall, the recent developments with Hensel Phelps, the SFO case study, and the March product upgrades indicate a period of meaningful commercial traction for Track3D, potentially solidifying its competitive position in the construction technology landscape.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION EXPANDS INNSBROOK OFFICE WITH LOCAL SUPPORT

DPR Construction, a leading regional construction firm, is set to expand its office space in Innsbrook by 9,100 square feet. The announcement comes as part of a strategic move to accommodate the company's growing workforce and enhance its operational capabilities. The expansion is supported by local incentives from Henrico County, which aims to attract and retain businesses in the area. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The new office space will allow DPR Construction to bring together teams and resources, fostering collaboration and innovation within the company. This growth is expected to create additional job opportunities in the region, further contributing to Henrico's economic development goals. Local officials have expressed optimism about the project, viewing it as a significant investment in the community's future.DPR Construction has been a prominent player in the construction industry, known for its commitment to quality and sustainability. The company's decision to expand its presence in Innsbrook reflects its confidence in the local market and the potential for continued growth. The support from Henrico County is part of broader efforts to enhance the business environment, making it easier for companies to invest and thrive.As part of the expansion, DPR Construction plans to implement modern design features and sustainable practices in the new office layout. This aligns with the company's mission to lead in sustainable construction practices while also providing a conducive work environment for its employees. The expansion is anticipated to be completed within the next year, further solidifying DPR Construction's commitment to the region.In addition to the expansion, DPR Construction has been actively involved in various community initiatives, underscoring its dedication to corporate social responsibility. As the company prepares for this new chapter, stakeholders are keenly watching how this expansion will impact both the firm and the local economy.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION APPOINTS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP

DPR Construction has announced the appointment of Jason Page as the new lead for its Special Services Group in Nashville. As of 1st April 2026, Page brings over 14 years of experience to the role, having joined DPR Construction in January 2012. His career has been marked by a versatile portfolio, encompassing projects from minor renovations to large-scale, complex developments.Since relocating to Nashville in April 2021, Jason Page has successfully spearheaded numerous corporate interior and entertainment projects, consistently delivering high-quality results under tight deadlines. His approach emphasizes a client-first leadership style, which has allowed him to cultivate strong relationships across various market sectors in the region. Page's passion for renovation and tenant improvement work has positioned him as a key player in DPR Construction's local operations.The DPR Special Services Group (SSG) plays a pivotal role in executing a diverse range of projects, from targeted improvements like parking garage stair installations to expansive interior buildouts for new office and entertainment spaces. Under Page's leadership, the SSG is expected to enhance its capabilities in meeting the evolving needs of clients and stakeholders in Nashville.In addition to project leadership, Jason Page has been actively involved in mentoring and coaching project teams within DPR Construction. He has shared best practices for executing quick-turn, high-impact work, fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation. His dedication to developing talent within the organization is seen as a valuable asset as DPR Construction continues to expand its footprint in Nashville.With Jason Page at the helm of the Special Services Group, DPR Construction is well-positioned to leverage his expertise and leadership as it navigates the competitive landscape of Nashville's construction market. The company's commitment to delivering exceptional service and quality projects remains steadfast, supported by Page's extensive experience and strategic vision.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK® CERTIFICATION AMID HIGH EMPLOYEE SATISFACTION

DPR Construction, a leader in the technical building sector, has secured Great Place To Work® Certification, with an impressive 97 percent of its employees affirming that it is a great place to work. This remarkable statistic is notably 40 points higher than the typical score given to workplaces across the United States. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, underscores DPR's commitment to fostering a workplace culture characterized by pride, belonging, and opportunity.George Pfeffer, CEO of DPR Construction, emphasized the significance of this certification, which reflects the sentiments of the employees who embody the company's culture. He stated, "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life." Pfeffer highlighted the company's focus on creating an inclusive environment where employees feel trusted, supported, and encouraged to grow, thus enabling them to perform at their best.DPR Construction's operational philosophy is built on empowering employees across its various projects to make decisions and share information openly. This approach encourages initiative, whether it involves innovating on complex tasks, stepping into new roles, or contributing ideas beyond formal job descriptions. Employees benefit from career paths aligned with their aspirations, supported by various learning and development programs.The research conducted by Great Place To Work® corroborates DPR's employee-centric strategy, revealing that a significant portion of the workforce feels a sense of pride in their contributions and a strong sense of belonging within the company. The certification further enhances DPR's reputation as an employer of choice, with additional accolades from U.S. News & World Report and Newsweek recognizing it as one of the best companies to work for, particularly in over 15 U.S. cities over the past year.DPR Construction has built a strong legacy since its founding in 1990, evolving into a multi-billion-dollar organization that specializes in complex and sustainable projects across various sectors. Its commitment to innovation, through the use of virtual design and construction and self-performing craft, positions DPR among the top contractors in the industry, employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide. For more details about DPR's culture and career opportunities, visit their official website.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMMENCES VERTICAL BUILD ON $900M GEISINGER EXPANSION

WILKES-BARRE, PA — Turner Construction has begun vertical construction on the $900 million expansion of Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center, marking a major milestone in one of northeastern Pennsylvania’s largest health care investments. The date of this announcement is 28th April 2026.After years of planning and foundational work, steel is now rising on the 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower. This new structure will increase the hospital's capacity from 356 to 500 beds and expand the number of operating rooms from 12 to 18. Additionally, the emergency department will be enlarged, supporting Geisinger’s goal of providing all private patient rooms. The facility is expected to open in the first quarter of 2028.David Kaminski, Vice President & Senior General Manager at Turner Construction, stated, “This milestone marks an important step forward in delivering expanded, modern health care access for families across northeastern Pennsylvania. We are proud to partner with Geisinger on a transformational project that will enhance patient care, support caregivers, and strengthen this community for decades to come.”The project is anticipated to support up to 3,800 construction jobs and generate approximately $1 billion in local economic impact. The onsite workforce is expected to grow from 150 to as many as 450 workers during peak activity. Upon completion, Geisinger plans to hire an additional 400 employees, further contributing to local job creation.Originally opened in 1981, the Geisinger Wyoming Valley campus has expanded over the decades to meet the growing demand for health services. This latest addition will bring advanced medical capabilities comparable to those found in major academic medical centers located in large metropolitan areas.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION MARKS KEY MILESTONE WITH ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER

FREDERICK, MD — Turner Construction Company celebrated the topping out of the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, marking the completion of the structural phase for the 72-megawatt facility. This significant milestone was achieved on the 28th of April 2026, less than five months after breaking ground, showcasing the effectiveness of early planning and strong collaboration among the project team.To date, the project has seen over 80,000 craft hours logged, with approximately 345 skilled workers contributing to its progress. "Topping out this facility represents more than completing the structural frame," stated Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner Construction Company. "It reflects the strength of our partnership with Aligned, the dedication of our trade partners, and Turner’s commitment to delivering this complex, high-performance data center safely and efficiently."The Aligned IAD-06 Data Center spans 450,000 square feet and is situated on a 75-acre campus designed to meet the increasing digital infrastructure demands of the region. The facility features a two-story structural steel design, an insulated precast exterior system, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling solution that combines both liquid and air-cooled technologies to optimize operational performance.The successful topping out of the IAD-06 Data Center underscores Turner Construction's expertise in managing complex projects in the data center sector, which is rapidly evolving to accommodate the surge in digital services. The collaborative efforts demonstrated by Turner and its partners are pivotal in ensuring that the project meets its ambitious timeline and quality standards.As the project moves into the next phases of development, stakeholders will be closely monitoring the advancements made by Turner Construction and Aligned. With the structural phase completed, the focus will shift to the interior fit-out and operational readiness of the facility, further solidifying its role in enhancing the region's digital infrastructure.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION SELECTED FOR AMBITIOUS WESTCOURT ORLANDO PROJECT

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, an ambitious development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a significant milestone in the project’s progression.The decision to partner with Turner Construction stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures, the project developer. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, noted that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, especially along Florida’s Space Coast, which includes coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches. Chapman recalled an early meeting where he expressed confidence in their ability to tackle the Westcourt project, saying, “If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us.”Turner Construction has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, focusing on pre-construction activities alongside JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Jeff Justen, vice president and general manager of Turner’s Orlando Business Unit, indicated that the early site activities have commenced, including utility mapping and surveying. These preparatory measures are critical for ensuring a smooth transition into the next phases of construction.The Westcourt project has faced some delays, shifting timelines due to financing challenges and fluctuations in construction costs. However, Chapman emphasized that involving Turner Construction early in the process allowed for valuable input on design and execution strategies. “We don’t want to design in a vacuum,” he said, highlighting the importance of contractor collaboration during the planning stages.Valued at approximately $500 million, the Westcourt development aims to create a vibrant mixed-use space featuring a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center. During peak construction, the project is expected to support around 800 workers on-site, contributing to the local job market in Central Florida. Chapman expressed excitement over the project’s complexity, stating that JMA Ventures is drawn to intricate developments, which informed their choice in partners like Turner Construction.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. [3 News That Matters]

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION SECURES SPOT ON FORTUNE 100 BEST COMPANIES TO WORK FOR

PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. has been recognized as one of the Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For in 2026. This acknowledgment comes as a testament to PCL's commitment to fostering a high-trust workplace environment, reflecting the company's dedication to employee satisfaction and engagement. The official announcement was made on 2nd April 2026.This marks the 17th time PCL Construction has earned a place on this prestigious list, achieving a notable rank of 91 this year. The recognition highlights PCL's sustained efforts in creating a supportive and empowering work environment, where employees feel valued and motivated to contribute to the company's success.Deron Brown, president and chief operating officer of PCL's U.S. operations, emphasized that the company's employee-owned structure plays a critical role in shaping its culture. He noted, 'Our people are directly invested in shaping a culture where they feel trusted, supported and excited about the future.' This approach not only enhances employee morale but also drives the overall performance of PCL Construction.PCL's recognition comes on the heels of a successful year, during which the company also ranked No. 11 on Engineering News-Record’s Top 400 Contractors list for 2025. This accolade underscores PCL Construction's leadership in executing complex projects across North America while maintaining its focus on employee well-being and satisfaction.The Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For list serves as a benchmark for organizational health and employee experience. Companies like PCL Construction, which consistently rank high on this list, often see a positive correlation between employee trust and business performance, reinforcing the belief that a supportive workplace is essential for long-term success.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION TACKLES COLORADO RIVER CRISIS WITH INNOVATIVE WATER SOLUTIONS

As the Colorado River faces unprecedented challenges, PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. is stepping up to provide innovative solutions aimed at water resilience. On the 26th of March 2026, the urgency surrounding the Colorado River's dwindling water supply became a focal point for communities across the Western United States, as they seek alternatives to secure their water futures.The Colorado River serves as a vital resource for millions, yet it is currently grappling with severe drought conditions. Lake Powell, for instance, is at an alarming 162 feet below full capacity, holding only 26% of its total water volume. The U.S. Bureau of Reclamation warns that if current conditions persist, Lake Mead could reach record lows within two years, prompting a critical reassessment of water management strategies across the basin states.PCL Construction is at the forefront of this reassessment, advocating for investments in water reuse infrastructure as a key strategy for bolstering water security. With projected U.S. investments in water reuse expected to average $47.1 billion over the next decade, PCL is positioned to support municipalities in capturing and reusing water to mitigate the effects of ongoing drought. Although adoption rates vary significantly among the seven Colorado River basin states, PCL's initiatives exemplify how infrastructure investments can enhance resilience in water supply systems.The urgency of the situation is underscored by the imminent expiration of current water management rules in the fall of 2026. As states grapple with the looming deadline to ratify Colorado River water allocation agreements, PCL Construction's projects, such as the Drought Pipeline Program in Phoenix, provide tangible examples of how communities can adapt to a drier future. This program has been crucial in ensuring the reliability of water supply for the rapidly growing Phoenix area, which has seen a 12% population increase over the last decade.Industry leaders, including PCL, recently gathered at the Colorado River Water Users Association Conference to discuss collaborative strategies for securing the river's future. The consensus was clear: a multifaceted approach is necessary to address the complexities of climate change and aging infrastructure. PCL Construction's commitment to advancing water reuse initiatives positions the company as a critical player in shaping sustainable water management practices that can withstand the challenges posed by the Colorado River's ongoing crisis.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ADVANCES $192M AI-READY DATA CENTER PROJECT

PCL Construction Services, Inc. and PCL Industrial Services, Inc., operating companies of PCL Construction, have reached structural completion on a $192 million, 22.5-megawatt data center, advancing one of the largest digital infrastructure projects currently underway in the Denver metropolitan area. On 19th March 2026, the companies celebrated this significant milestone that underscores their commitment to delivering state-of-the-art facilities designed for high-capacity digital workloads.Located on a 17-acre site southeast of Denver, the project is being delivered by PCL’s Minneapolis Buildings group in collaboration with the Denver team, combining longstanding client relationships with local market expertise. The data center is specifically designed to support enterprise-scale, compute-intensive workloads, including artificial intelligence and graphics processing unit applications, as demand for high-capacity digital infrastructure continues to grow across the region.PCL’s scope of work includes the phased construction of five steel-frame colocation data halls totaling approximately 277,000 square feet, allowing for efficient onboarding of new tenants while maintaining flexibility for future expansion. The project also features a dedicated administration building with office space for personnel and on-site operations teams. This design aims to accommodate high-density deployments, aligning performance needs with modern sustainability and energy-efficiency requirements.The project includes comprehensive electrical infrastructure to support continuous, high-performance operations, from permanent and temporary power systems to sitewide distribution, security, and communications pathways. Key work includes installing and integrating major power and cooling equipment that enables reliable, high-density data center operations. PCL Industrial Services, Inc. is self-performing the core electrical work while coordinating specialized partners for testing and life-safety systems.PCL Construction’s extensive experience in delivering mission-critical and data center facilities across the United States is critical on complex, high-density projects like this, according to Ryan Schmidt, vice president and district manager of Denver Buildings. The project teams recently marked the topping-out ceremony, celebrating the dedication, craftsmanship, and collaboration that will transition the development into interior build-out and systems installation, with construction scheduled for completion in summer 2027.

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE SECURES JOBS GRANT FOR MAJOR HQ EXPANSION

In a significant move to bolster local economic development, the Jefferson County Commission has approved a jobs grant for Brasfield & Gorrie, a prominent construction firm headquartered in Birmingham. On 25th March 2026, the Commission unanimously passed the resolution, which supports the company’s expansion plans that include a new office tower, a parking deck expansion, a training and event center, and a wellness center.Commission President Jimmie Stephens emphasized the importance of Brasfield & Gorrie to the region, noting that the company has been a stalwart presence in Birmingham since its inception. "They’ve chosen to remain here although they are a worldwide company," he remarked, highlighting the firm's commitment to the local economy.The expansion represents a substantial investment of $64,584,500 from Brasfield & Gorrie, with $2,884,500 allocated for land acquisition, $56,100,000 designated for construction, and $5,600,000 earmarked for equipment. This development is expected to create 80 new jobs, each offering an hourly wage of $39.51. The county’s jobs grant, capped at $240,000, is designed to incentivize this growth.In return for its investment, Jefferson County anticipates a return of $8,000 to the general fund and $1,011,773 to the education fund, illustrating the potential benefits of supporting local businesses like Brasfield & Gorrie. Stephens expressed gratitude for the confidence the company has shown in the county, vowing to maintain a strong partnership moving forward.As the economic landscape continues to evolve, the expansion of Brasfield & Gorrie underscores the importance of local support for sustainable growth. The firm’s decision to invest in Birmingham not only strengthens its operational base but also contributes positively to the broader community.

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE UNVEILS $64.5 MILLION EXPANSION PLANS FOR BIRMINGHAM HQ

Brasfield & Gorrie, one of Birmingham’s largest construction firms, is planning a major expansion at its national headquarters, a move company leaders say reflects continued growth and a long-term commitment to the city. The company presented plans to the Birmingham City Council committee on 10th March 2026 for a new office building and parking expansion at its headquarters campus along 7th Avenue South.The proposal outlines a project expected to bring millions of dollars in investment and dozens of new jobs to the city. Brasfield & Gorrie plans to construct a new four-story office building totaling roughly 80,000 square feet. According to company representatives, the first floor will serve as an event and training center designed to host gatherings and professional development opportunities, featuring a large banquet room, an auditorium, and several meeting and training rooms.The remaining three floors are planned for office space that will support Brasfield & Gorrie’s expanding national operations. Company leaders say the additional space will allow them to accommodate new employees while leaving room for future growth. In addition to the new building, Brasfield & Gorrie plans to add a fourth level to its existing parking deck, creating approximately 125 additional parking spaces for employees and visitors.As part of the expansion, Brasfield & Gorrie plans to create 80 new full-time jobs headquartered in Birmingham, with an expected average salary of about $82,000. During the council committee discussion, company leaders emphasized that the new roles will help support the firm’s operations across the country while keeping its headquarters firmly rooted in Birmingham. They stated, 'It will be jobs headquartered here in Birmingham to support our field and our operations. We’re really growing, and we’re committed to staying in Birmingham.'The project represents an estimated $64.5 million investment. Under the proposed agreement, the City of Birmingham would provide financial incentives of up to $80,000 to support the project. The proposal received approval from the council committee and will now move to the full Birmingham City Council for final consideration. If approved, company leaders say construction on the expansion could be completed by late 2027.

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE RECEIVES $12M TAX INCENTIVE FOR JEFFERSON COUNTY PROJECT

Birmingham-based construction firm Brasfield & Gorrie has secured a substantial economic incentive package from state officials, aimed at bolstering its $65 million project in Jefferson County. On 20th February 2026, the Alabama Department of Commerce approved nearly $12 million in tax credits for the company, which includes $9.69 million in investment tax credits and $2.24 million in jobs tax credits.The incentives are part of a strategic initiative to promote economic development in the region and are expected to play a significant role in the execution of Brasfield & Gorrie's ambitious project. This funding will not only support the immediate development but also contribute to job creation and local investment, which aligns with the state's broader economic goals.Brasfield & Gorrie, a privately held firm recognized for its extensive portfolio in construction and project management, has been a key player in Alabama's construction landscape. The approval of these tax credits underscores the state's commitment to fostering growth within its local businesses, particularly in the construction sector, which has faced challenges amid fluctuating economic conditions.The $64.6 million project will likely enhance Brasfield & Gorrie's operational capabilities and expand its service offerings, further solidifying its market position. As the company moves forward with the development, stakeholders will be keenly observing how these incentives translate into tangible benefits for both Brasfield & Gorrie and the Jefferson County community.In conclusion, the Alabama Department of Commerce's support through this tax incentive package marks a significant milestone for Brasfield & Gorrie, reinforcing its role in driving economic progress in the region. The anticipated outcomes of this project will not only reflect on the company's growth trajectory but also on the overall economic vitality of Birmingham and its surrounding areas.

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION UNVEILS FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ENHANCE MANUFACTURING CAPABILITIES

JE Dunn Construction has launched its new off-site manufacturing initiative, Form Off-Site Solutions(TradeMark), designed to specialize in Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex construction components. This strategic move comes as the company seeks to capitalize on its century-long legacy in Kansas City and beyond. The introduction of Form Off-Site Solutions is positioned as a response to the growing demand for advanced manufacturing capabilities, especially in a rapidly evolving construction landscape. The launch date of this initiative is 6th April 2026.Led by Off-Site Manufacturing Director Nick Effenheim, Form Off-Site Solutions brings together skilled craftspeople, advanced automation, and innovative strategies to design and produce specialized components for a diverse array of stakeholders, including owners, general contractors, trade partners, and original equipment manufacturers. The offerings are categorized into four distinct product segments: metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies, ensuring that various market needs are met with tailored solutions.The end-to-end service model of Form Off-Site Solutions addresses the multifaceted challenges inherent in construction projects. By providing capabilities that range from estimating and design to manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, JE Dunn Construction aims to deliver improved installation efficiency, enhanced project safety and quality, and maximized cost certainty. This comprehensive approach is intended to alleviate labor shortages and accelerate project timelines, critical factors in today's construction environment.Effenheim emphasizes the collaborative nature of Form's operations, stating, "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and focus on getting their ideas, concepts, and design into a manufactured environment is something that sets us apart." This commitment to collaboration and innovation is echoed by JE Dunn President and CEO Gordon Lansford, who notes that the development of Form Off-Site Solutions represents a significant advancement in the company's ongoing quest to drive innovation for its clients.With production facilities located in Kansas City and Nashville, Form Off-Site Solutions is poised to serve clients on a national scale. The company's focus on innovation and strategic thinking is a cornerstone of its operations, aimed at enhancing project safety and quality while also addressing labor shortages in the industry. As JE Dunn Construction continues to push the boundaries of off-site manufacturing, the launch of Form Off-Site Solutions marks a pivotal step in its mission to deliver projects with greater efficiency and certainty.

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION CELEBRATES TOPPING-OUT MILESTONE FOR WORKFORCE CENTER

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — Construction has reached a key milestone on the Northland Workforce Development Center, as JE Dunn Construction and project partners recently celebrated the topping out of the new facility. The event, held on 19th March 2026, marked the placement of the final structural steel beam for the project, signaling significant progress on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story building designed to expand access to career and technical education across the Northland region.Prior to the beam’s installation, attendees participated in a traditional beam-signing ceremony, adding their names and messages to a steel beam bearing project and partner logos. This moment underscored the collaboration among the Platte County R-3 School District, partner school districts, project stakeholders, and the broader community in advancing a shared workforce development vision. Brad Hokanson, Project Executive at JE Dunn Construction, emphasized the importance of this collaboration, stating, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The Northland Workforce Development Center is designed to support programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services. Once complete, the facility will expand the capacity of Northland Career Center and its partners, serving both high school students and adult learners seeking industry-aligned training and credentials. Planned program spaces include flexible labs and classrooms, hands-on training environments, and technology-rich areas that reflect real-world workplace conditions.Located in the Northland region of Kansas City, the project is progressing on schedule, with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening anticipated following the structural milestone. Brian Noller, Executive Director of Business Services and NWDC Campaign Lead at Platte County School District, highlighted the collaborative efforts, stating, 'With support from the State of Missouri, local municipalities, businesses and industries, individual investors, and community foundations, we are making a long-term investment in the success of our students and the future of our region.'JE Dunn is serving as construction manager for the project, working in partnership with the school district and design firm Clark & Enersen. The project team has emphasized safety, schedule adherence, and coordination throughout construction, while maintaining communication with stakeholders and minimizing disruption to nearby facilities. The Northland Workforce Development Center represents a regional investment in workforce readiness, aligning educational infrastructure with evolving industry needs and supporting long-term economic development in the Kansas City area.

+ CHRIS CHELETTE REJOINS JE DUNN TO BOLSTER HEALTHCARE SECTOR

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025.During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region.In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction.Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively.As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

+ GILBANE DEVELOPMENT ELEVATES ANDREAS VLAHAKIS TO LEAD CAPITAL MARKETS

Gilbane Development has appointed Andreas Vlahakis as head of capital markets, a move aimed at strengthening the firm’s investment strategies. As of 28th April 2026, Vlahakis will operate from the company’s New York City office, bringing with him a wealth of experience in real estate capital markets across various asset classes, particularly in ground-up multifamily and student housing investments.In his new role at Gilbane Development, Vlahakis will be responsible for refining and executing the firm's funding strategy. Reporting directly to James Patchett, president and CEO, he will play a pivotal role in setting the capital strategy, making crucial investment decisions, and developing strong relationships with funding and equity partners. His expertise will also extend to leading transaction structuring and negotiations, positioning Gilbane to capitalize on emerging market opportunities.Before joining Gilbane Development, Vlahakis served as managing director for capital markets at Thor Equities, where he was instrumental in leading equity-raising efforts for the firm's various projects and partnerships. His prior experience also includes a role as senior vice president and chief of staff at Thor Equities, further underscoring his capability in navigating complex financial landscapes. Vlahakis's extensive background equips him with the insight necessary to enhance Gilbane's standing within the competitive real estate sector.Gilbane Development, known for its commitment to quality and innovation in real estate, is strategically positioning itself for growth with this significant leadership change. By appointing Vlahakis, the firm aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its capital strategies and investment portfolio, particularly in sectors that align with evolving market demands. Stakeholders and investors will be keenly observing how Vlahakis's leadership will influence Gilbane’s future endeavors.As the real estate market continues to evolve, the importance of strong leadership in capital markets becomes increasingly evident. Gilbane Development's decision to bring on Andreas Vlahakis reflects a proactive approach to securing its capital needs and driving forward its ambitious growth plans. Investors will likely be curious to see how Vlahakis's experience will translate into tangible results for the firm.

+ WKU ANNOUNCES GILBANE PARTNERSHIP, PAVING THE WAY FOR NEW DEVELOPMENTS

The Western Kentucky University (WKU) Student Government Association (SGA) has officially announced the approval of WKU's public-private partnership with Gilbane. This significant development indicates the beginning of a collaborative effort aimed at enhancing student facilities and resources. As part of the partnership, which was formally approved by WKU’s Board of Regents on 11th December 2025, Gilbane will design and develop a new residence hall, intended to replace the outdated Douglas Keen and Hugh Poland Halls.Following the recent approval from the Kentucky Legislature on 6th April 2026, WKU is poised to commence construction on the new residence halls. SGA Senator Jakob Barker confirmed that the partnership with Gilbane is now fully authorized, stating, "WKU is now in the clear to start building the new residence halls." This development not only addresses housing needs but also reflects WKU's commitment to improving the overall student experience.In addition to the housing initiative, the SGA has passed several legislative measures aimed at enhancing student resources. Among these is Resolution 7-26-S, which supports the installation of a crosswalk at a crucial intersection near student housing. The resolution highlights the safety concerns of students who currently navigate busy streets to access essential facilities, advocating for collaboration with the Kentucky Department of Transportation for this project.Further addressing financial constraints faced by students, Bill 17-26-S was also approved, establishing the Textbooks for Toppers reimbursement program. This initiative will provide $50 reimbursements for students purchasing required course materials, a move that reflects the SGA's recognition of the financial challenges many students encounter. Additionally, other bills passed will provide essential academic supplies, such as Scantrons and lab goggles, ensuring that students have access to necessary materials without added financial burden.As WKU moves forward with its partnership with Gilbane and implements these new funding initiatives, the SGA's commitment to improving the student experience is clear. The collaboration with Gilbane not only aims to enhance student housing but also signifies a broader strategy to address various student needs and concerns, fostering a supportive academic environment.

+ GILBANE LAUNCHES LEASING FOR $135M AFFORDABLE HOUSING PROJECT IN D.C.

WASHINGTON, D.C. — Gilbane Development has begun leasing the second of its three-phase redevelopment at 7th Street and Barnaby Road in Southeast Washington, D.C. As of 3rd April 2026, Phase II of Barnaby & 7th includes 229 units within four new residential buildings: The Stoneview, The Solomon, The Marte, and The Lewis.Construction of these buildings is slated for completion by summer 2026. The four communities feature a mix of studio to three-bedroom units serving households earning 50 and 80 percent of the area median income (AMI). Notably, twenty percent of the apartments will be set aside as Permanent Supportive Housing (PSH) units, which will be managed by the district’s Department of Homeless Services.Phase I of Barnaby & 7th consisted of The Upland, which was completed in spring 2025 and includes 169 apartments, with 34 designated as PSH units. The income restrictions for this phase ranged from 30 to 50 percent of AMI. The third phase of development is expected to include approximately 100 additional rental units in one multifamily building, bringing the project to a grand total of 498 units upon completion.During the construction of Phase II, Gilbane also built a new pedestrian walkway designed to connect all six buildings of the larger development. According to the District of Columbia Housing Finance Agency, the total project cost for Phase II was approximately $135 million, underscoring Gilbane's commitment to affordable housing in the region.As the project progresses, it is anticipated to significantly contribute to the availability of affordable housing options in Washington, D.C., while also enhancing community connectivity and providing essential support services for residents in need.

+ CARLY REINER CACERES JOINS CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP AS HR DIRECTOR

Carly Reiner Caceres has been appointed as the Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction Group, bringing a wealth of experience in people operations and talent strategy. On the 29th of April 2026, Caceres officially began her new role, where she will be instrumental in supporting national campus recruiting initiatives aimed at bolstering early-career talent pipelines for one of the largest building and infrastructure companies in the United States.Prior to her appointment at Clark Construction Group, Caceres served as the Vice President of People Operations at Proper, where she was pivotal in shaping the HR strategy and operational support within a hybrid work environment. Her extensive background includes significant roles at MILLER as the Director of Internal Operations and more than nine years at Accenture, where she excelled in various capacities including Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Caceres’ career trajectory showcases her expertise in full-cycle recruiting, stakeholder management, and employer branding, which will be crucial as Clark Construction Group seeks to attract top talent from universities across the nation. Her previous experience managing campus recruiting strategies at Grant Thornton LLP further solidifies her capabilities in building robust internship programs and enhancing student engagement initiatives.Clark Construction Group, founded in 1906, has established a strong reputation for delivering value across major markets, including education and infrastructure. With a commitment to community strengthening and project excellence, the company is poised to benefit from Caceres' strategic insights and leadership in HR, particularly in nurturing the next generation of professionals in the construction industry.Caceres’ early career involved internships and roles with various organizations, including Charleston Magazine and the Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum, which helped her build a diverse skill set in communications and external relations. As she embarks on this new chapter at Clark Construction Group, her multifaceted background is expected to contribute significantly to the company's growth and talent development objectives.

+ CLARK CONTRACTS SECURES MULTI-TRADES FRAMEWORK WITH RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL

Clark Contracts has been appointed to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades contractors framework, enhancing the delivery of repair, maintenance, and refurbishment services to domestic properties across the local authority area. This announcement was made on 11th February 2026.The framework, which is set to run until 2027, encompasses a variety of multi-trade works. This will enable Renfrewshire Council to effectively manage both responsive and planned maintenance services across its housing stock, ensuring that local residents receive prompt and reliable service.Clark Contracts is already an established player in the public sector, holding several framework agreements that include Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework. Additionally, the company is involved with the Crown Commercial Service’s Construction Framework and various Scottish Procurement Alliance frameworks focusing on Public Buildings, New Build Housing, and more.Gordon Cunningham, managing director at Clark Contracts, expressed enthusiasm about the new appointment, saying, "Building on our strong track record of delivering more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over the past three years, we are pleased to have been appointed to Renfrewshire Council’s framework. As a Paisley-based business, we are proud to be supporting the council and delivering refurbishment and maintenance services that directly benefit local communities."The addition of this framework is expected to bolster Clark Contracts' already significant presence in the public sector, as the company continues to collaborate with various councils and institutions, including the University of Glasgow and Glasgow City Council, to deliver essential services.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP ADVANCES ₱4-B MIXED-USE HUB AT THE VILLAGES

CLARK FREEPORT – A ₱4-billion mixed-use residential and commercial development in The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination here, has been given the green light with the approval by the Clark Development Corp. of an amendment to PKR Corp.’s lease agreement on 13th December 2025. This ambitious project underscores Clark Construction Group's aggressive strategy to attract significant investments and fortify its reputation as Asia’s Leading Meetings and Conference Destination, a title recently bestowed by the World Travel Awards.The revised lease agreement provides PKR Corp. with a 30-month timeline to complete construction and incorporates stringent performance safeguards to ensure timely project delivery. Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera, president and CEO of Clark Development Corp. (CDC), emphasized that the amendment reflects the organization's commitment to fostering investor confidence and efficient execution of projects. "With this agreement, we ensure that it is a good place for everyone to go and with our coordination, we want to be able to maintain that area [The Villages], an area of preference, where people can reside and really be part of the community," Devanadera stated.PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung expressed gratitude for the partnership with CDC, stating, "We are honored to take part in Clark’s growth and with CDC as our partner, we will deliver this development in Clark." PKR Corp. operates under the umbrella of Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean firm known for its expertise in building, civil, residential, and infrastructure projects. The company has received accolades for safety, technology, and workplace practices from various esteemed organizations including the Korea Expressway Corp. and the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy.This mixed-use development is part of CDC’s broader strategy to expedite investments, promote well-planned communities, and establish a business-friendly environment that appeals to both global and local investors. The focus on creating integrated lifestyle hubs aligns with the increasing demand for residential options that complement commercial and recreational spaces, positioning Clark as an attractive destination for future developments.Clark Construction Group's proactive approach in securing this project represents a significant step forward in the region's economic landscape, potentially catalyzing further investments and developments. As the construction timeline unfolds, stakeholders will be keenly observing how this initiative contributes to the overall growth and vibrancy of The Villages and the Clark Freeport Zone.
