ACTIVITIES:

Cabot Corporation is a global leader in specialty chemicals and performance materials, headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts, and listed on the New York Stock Exchange. The company's core business activities focus on the production and supply of advanced materials such as carbon black, which is critical for tires, industrial rubber, and plastics; fumed silica for adhesives, coatings, and electronics; and specialty additives for energy storage and inkjet applications. These offerings serve diverse industries including automotive, construction, and electronics, emphasizing innovation in sustainable and high-performance solutions. The fastest-growing segments include battery materials and sustainable products, fueled by rising demand for electric vehicles, renewable energy technologies, and eco-friendly alternatives. With a commitment to research and development, Cabot maintains a strong global footprint, operating in over 20 countries and generating revenue through strategic partnerships and operational efficiency.

MARKET POSITION:

Cabot Corporation (NYSE:CBT), the NYSE's 19th largest Chemicals company by market cap, increased $3.31 (or 4.4%) in the trailing 12 months to May 05, 2026 to close at $78.93. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 27.6% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -23.3%. Average daily volume of 428,362 shares in the trailing year was 1.1 times average daily volume in the previous year.

urn:contentItem:6JHJ-SYF3-SGBR-T1H3-00000-00SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION - THE RECENT SECTOR HEADLINES WITH PEERS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:57ZLexisNexisSTOCKSUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION - THE RECENT SECTOR HEADLINES WITH PEERS

DASHBOARD: SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION

PermID5000317077Websitehttp://www.suffolk.com/IndustryConstruction & Engineering

ACTIVITIES:

Suffolk Construction, founded in 1982 by John Fish in Boston, Massachusetts, is a leading U.S. general contractor. With $5.5 billion in revenue and 2,500 employees, Suffolk delivers complex projects in healthcare, education, and commercial sectors. Notable works include Bostons Millennium Tower and Miamis Brickell City Centre. Employing advanced technologies like BIM and virtual design, Suffolk enhances project efficiency and safety. The company operates 10 offices nationwide, with a strong Northeast presence. Suffolks commitment to sustainability includes LEED-certified projects and eco-friendly practices. Its employee-centric culture, with robust training and diversity initiatives, earned it a spot on Fortunes Best Workplaces list. Suffolk continues to innovate, focusing on smart buildings and urban development.

+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION'S LANE REOPENING ON ROUTE 58 SIGNALS PROGRESS

SUFFOLK, Va. — Motorists on U.S. Route 58 near downtown Suffolk will soon see relief as the Virginia Department of Transportation (VDOT) begins removing a long-term lane closure starting as early as Monday night. As of 5th May 2026, the right lane closure on Route 58 westbound just east of the downtown exit will be reopened, allowing all three lanes of westbound traffic to flow freely once again.The lane closure has been in effect since March, coinciding with the ongoing Southeastern Public Service Authority interchange project managed by Suffolk Construction, which began in August 2024. The completion of this project aims to facilitate a new ramp and flyover for eastbound traffic, enabling vehicles to access the regional landfill without crossing over westbound lanes, thereby improving traffic safety and efficiency.While the reopening of the lane is a welcome change, motorists should be aware that some temporary overnight closures may occur as crews work to remove the temporary barriers. These closures will take place from 8 p.m. to 5 a.m., depending on weather conditions and scheduling factors. Long-term shoulder closures remain in effect in both directions, and intermittent lane closures or brief stoppages may still be possible as the project progresses.The speed limit through this construction zone is set at 50 miles per hour, and speed ticket cameras are actively monitoring both directions to ensure compliance. Motorists are encouraged to stay informed about the ongoing work and any potential disruptions by visiting VDOT's dedicated project website.As Suffolk Construction continues to make strides on this critical infrastructure project, the reopening of Route 58 is a significant milestone that reflects the company's commitment to enhancing transportation in the region. Further updates regarding the project will be available as work continues.

+ VMO2 SECURES 10-YEAR SOLAR ENERGY PACT WITH SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION

Virgin Media O2 (VMO2) is taking a significant step towards enhancing its renewable energy footprint by entering into a 10-year Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with solar power provider Egg Power. This strategic collaboration will allow VMO2 to source energy from Egg's 70MW solar farm located in Suffolk, which is under construction and anticipated to commence power generation in 2027.The agreement, announced on 1st May 2026, is set to supply approximately 5% of VMO2's total energy demands, marking a noteworthy addition to the company's sustainability initiatives. Egg Power, as a natural energy partner for VMO2, is particularly aligned with the company’s goals, given that both entities are under the ownership of Liberty Global. This partnership is expected to significantly aid VMO2 in its pursuit of achieving Net Zero carbon emissions by 2040.Mark Hardman, Director of Finance Operations at VMO2, emphasized the importance of this agreement, stating, “This agreement with Egg Power is the latest step in Virgin Media O2’s journey to achieve net zero emissions by the end of 2040. We’re committed to growing and operating our business in a way that’s good for people and the planet, where we’re cutting carbon, securing renewable energy on a long-term basis, and sourcing renewable energy generation from the UK.”This new PPA with Egg Power complements a previous 10-year agreement VMO2 entered into for wind power with The Renewables Infrastructure Group last year. Together, these two agreements will enable VMO2 to source around 20% of its energy from renewable sources through Power Purchase Agreements, reinforcing the company's commitment to sustainability.As VMO2 continues to advance its renewable energy strategy, this latest partnership not only addresses immediate energy needs but also aligns with broader environmental goals. The growing reliance on renewable energy sources is becoming increasingly critical for companies like VMO2 as they navigate the challenges of climate change and strive for sustainable operational practices.

+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION BRACES FOR $897M CAPITAL SPENDING PROPOSAL

Suffolk County Executive Edward P. Romaine has unveiled a capital budget proposal totaling $897 million for 2027, which aims to address critical infrastructure needs within Suffolk County. The proposal, revealed on 24th April 2026, is part of a broader three-year capital program that includes approximately $3 billion in investments, maintaining a similar figure to a previously adopted plan.This proposed budget marks a 15% increase compared to the adopted 2026 capital budget, emphasizing long-term public works projects. Romaine highlighted that the budget focuses on "infrastructure needs that have been neglected for so long," framing it as an investment in Suffolk County's future. The capital budget operates independently from the operating budget, which is anticipated in the fall.Among the key components of the 2027 budget are $208 million allocated for sewer upgrades, $200 million dedicated to a new transportation hub near the Ronkonkoma Long Island Rail Road station, and $128 million for various road, bridge, sidewalk, and public transit improvements. A significant aspect of the proposal is the $185 million earmarked for a new medical examiner building, which will feature advanced ventilation, security, and lab facilities. The current building, dating back to the late 1980s, has been deemed beyond repair.Construction on the new medical examiner facility is set to commence in 2027 following a $20 million bond authorized in February 2025 for design and engineering costs. The project is projected to be completed by 2029. Additionally, the budget outlines plans for the new Smith Point Bridge, which will enhance connectivity between Shirley and Fire Island, funded primarily through federal aid.In introducing the budget, Romaine, a Republican in his third year as County Executive, stressed its role in supporting farmland preservation, expanding recreational assets, and promoting growth in key economic areas. Presiding Officer Anthony Piccirillo has indicated that a working group will be established to review the proposed budget, with meetings scheduled to start in May following an analysis by the legislature's budget review office.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BALFOUR BEATTY [3 News That Matters]

+ BALFOUR BEATTY ELEVATES TRENT JOHNSON TO LEAD AVIATION OPERATIONS IN THE CAROLINAS

Balfour Beatty has promoted Trent Johnson to Vice President of operations in the Carolinas, effective immediately. Johnson's new role will see him leading and expanding the company’s aviation sector across North and South Carolina, building on a career that spans over 19 years in the construction industry.As of 1st April 2026, Trent Johnson’s promotion is a reflection of his extensive experience, including more than 12 and a half years with Balfour Beatty. During his tenure, he has successfully contributed to the delivery of 22 aviation projects, ranging from targeted renovations to complex, multi-phase programmes. His leadership has been pivotal in enhancing Balfour Beatty’s aviation presence, notably at Raleigh-Durham International Airport (RDU) and Albert J. Ellis Airport.In his new capacity, Johnson will continue to support Balfour Beatty’s existing aviation clients while expanding the company’s footprint at additional regional and international airports throughout the Carolinas. His focus will remain on assisting project teams in navigating the complexities of active airport environments, while prioritizing safety, operational continuity, and stakeholder coordination.Jeff Sandeen, President of Balfour Beatty in the Carolinas, commented on Johnson’s promotion, stating, “Trent has built a strong reputation as a trusted leader and client partner within our local aviation operations. His ability to guide teams through the complexities of active airport environments has been a key driver of our success in the region.” Sandeen expressed confidence that Johnson will play a crucial role in shaping the next phase of growth for Balfour Beatty’s aviation portfolio.Trent Johnson holds a degree in Construction Engineering Management from North Carolina State University and is also known for his community involvement, particularly with the Make-A-Wish Foundation. His steady leadership style and collaborative approach have helped Balfour Beatty deliver projects that align with long-term regional growth and infrastructure priorities.

+ MAINMARK UK JOINS BALFOUR BEATTY'S CP7 FRAMEWORK FOR RAIL INFRASTRUCTURE

Ground engineering specialist Mainmark UK has secured a place on the Balfour Beatty plc CP7 Framework, positioning the company to support the delivery of key rail infrastructure works across Network Rail’s latest five-year investment programme. This investment period, known as Control Period 7 (CP7), spans from 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2029, with an allocation of approximately £43–45 billion across Great Britain.The CP7 programme focuses on maintaining, renewing, and modernising the rail network to deliver a safer, more reliable, and more sustainable railway. Investment priorities during CP7 include infrastructure maintenance and renewals, digital signalling and technology, safety improvements, and initiatives aimed at enhancing both freight and passenger performance across the network.As part of the CP7 Framework, contractors will engage in a variety of works aligned with Network Rail’s Strategic Business Plan. These activities will encompass low-complexity emergency callouts, planned preventative maintenance, and small-scale civil engineering schemes. Mainmark UK will be involved in geotechnical response works, bridge repairs, structural integrity projects, platform refurbishments, and drainage clearance, among others.Tom Kavanagh, General Manager at Mainmark UK, expressed enthusiasm about the collaboration with Balfour Beatty, stating, “We’re looking forward to collaborating with Balfour Beatty to help deliver critical infrastructure works across the network during the CP7 period. Being selected to support Balfour Beatty on this framework is a fantastic opportunity for our team and aligns perfectly with our growth plans.”Mainmark is known for its innovative ground engineering solutions, particularly its Teretek® resin injection technology, which stabilises weak ground conditions and fills subsurface voids without extensive excavation. This technology is crucial for maintaining the integrity of rail infrastructure, especially as heavier freight loads and severe weather events put increasing pressure on railway assets. With its appointment to the CP7 Framework, Mainmark UK is set to play a vital role in ensuring the resilience and reliability of the UK's rail network.

+ BALFOUR BEATTY'S GROWTH FUELED BY RECORD ORDER BOOK

Balfour Beatty PLC has reported impressive growth, driven by its robust performance in the construction sector, particularly in power transmission projects. The company’s market valuation has soared nearly two-thirds over the past year, a noteworthy achievement for a FTSE 250 constituent. This growth trajectory is largely attributed to a record order book and a 9 percent increase in underlying earnings per share, which reached 47.6 pence.On 11th March 2026, the construction group revealed that its top-line growth was significantly supported by the increasing demand for power transmission in the UK and a surge in building projects in the US. Operating profits within Balfour Beatty's support services segment saw a remarkable increase of one-third, amounting to £122 million, alongside an 80 basis point rise in the underlying margin to 8.5 percent. The company’s UK construction division achieved its margin targets ahead of schedule, underscoring its operational efficiency.In the US market, Balfour Beatty has capitalized on rising building volumes although it faced challenges due to cost overruns in a project within its Civils arm. The dual market strategy appears to be paying off, as the company navigates through these hurdles while maintaining a focus on profitability. The expansion of infrastructure projects, particularly in power transmission, has provided a solid foundation for growth, enabling Balfour Beatty to solidify its position in the competitive construction landscape.Market analysts remain optimistic about Balfour Beatty’s future prospects, considering its substantial order book which continues to grow amidst a backdrop of increasing infrastructure investment. The company’s strategic initiatives to enhance operational margins and deliver projects on time have positioned it well for sustained growth in the coming quarters. Investors are likely to monitor Balfour Beatty’s performance closely as it seeks to leverage its strengths in both the UK and US markets.As Balfour Beatty continues to capitalize on the opportunities presented by the expanding power grid and overall construction demand, it remains crucial for stakeholders to assess the potential impacts of rising costs and project management challenges. Overall, the company’s recent performance indicates a strong recovery trajectory, making it a noteworthy player in the construction sector as it heads into the next fiscal year.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO [3 News That Matters]

+ TRACK3D SECURES MAJOR PARTNERSHIP WITH HENSEL PHELPS FOR CONSTRUCTION TECHNOLOGY

Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, has marked a significant milestone by securing a multi-year agreement with Hensel Phelps, expanding its collaboration from a single pilot to the deployment of Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform across more than 200 active projects. This announcement was made on 1st April 2026, emphasizing Track3D's growing influence in the construction sector.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize the use of Track3D for portfolio-wide progress monitoring, indicating strong confidence in the platform’s scalability and effectiveness across diverse construction projects. By utilizing automated progress tracking, Hensel Phelps aims to identify on-site issues earlier, which can significantly reduce delays and enhance schedule reliability, especially in complex builds.A notable case study from San Francisco International Airport highlighted the successful application of Track3D’s technology on a challenging infrastructure project. Project leaders reported a shift from reactive issue resolution to proactive decision-making, facilitated by 360deg site walks and the involvement of existing field personnel, thereby minimizing the need for major workflow changes. This deployment at SFO reinforces Track3D's relevance in high-value infrastructure work and solidifies its position in the reality intelligence market.In addition to the partnership with Hensel Phelps, Track3D has introduced a March update to its platform that focuses on large-enterprise oversight and workflow efficiency. Key enhancements include an admin dashboard and Groups functionality, which are designed to provide major contractors with centralized portfolio visibility and streamlined user management. Furthermore, the new capture-free progress tracking feature allows teams to document job site status directly on floorplans, promoting continuous oversight.Track3D is also intensifying its go-to-market strategy by targeting specialty contractors, with plans to participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026. The company aims to showcase tools that automate production tracking and reduce manual reporting for drywall and ceiling professionals. Collectively, these developments—ranging from the Hensel Phelps portfolio rollout to enterprise-focused product enhancements—underscore Track3D’s strengthening competitive position in construction technology and may pave the way for sustainable growth in the future.

+ MICAH CHOY STEPS UP AS PROJECT SUPERINTENDENT AT HENSEL PHELPS

Micah (Pono) Choy has been promoted to project superintendent at Hensel Phelps, overseeing the Hawaii Department of Education’s Regional Kitchen – North Central Oahu project in Wahiawa. As of 1st April 2026, Choy's promotion marks a significant milestone in his career with the company, which he joined in 2018.During his tenure at Hensel Phelps, Choy has played a pivotal role in various major projects across the aviation, wastewater, and federal market sectors in Hawaii and Guam. His collaborative leadership style has garnered recognition, allowing him to build strong partnerships across teams and effectively drive project outcomes.Choy's promotion to project superintendent reflects Hensel Phelps' commitment to developing talent within the organization. His experience and expertise will be instrumental in managing the Regional Kitchen project, which is critical for enhancing educational facilities in Hawaii.The construction industry continues to evolve, and Hensel Phelps is positioning itself for future growth by promoting leaders like Choy. + PCL CONSTRUCTION TACKLES COLORADO RIVER CRISIS WITH INNOVATIVE WATER SOLUTIONS

As the Colorado River faces unprecedented challenges, PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. is stepping up to provide innovative solutions aimed at water resilience. On the 26th of March 2026, the urgency surrounding the Colorado River's dwindling water supply became a focal point for communities across the Western United States, as they seek alternatives to secure their water futures.The Colorado River serves as a vital resource for millions, yet it is currently grappling with severe drought conditions. Lake Powell, for instance, is at an alarming 162 feet below full capacity, holding only 26% of its total water volume. The U.S. Bureau of Reclamation warns that if current conditions persist, Lake Mead could reach record lows within two years, prompting a critical reassessment of water management strategies across the basin states.PCL Construction is at the forefront of this reassessment, advocating for investments in water reuse infrastructure as a key strategy for bolstering water security. With projected U.S. investments in water reuse expected to average $47.1 billion over the next decade, PCL is positioned to support municipalities in capturing and reusing water to mitigate the effects of ongoing drought. Although adoption rates vary significantly among the seven Colorado River basin states, PCL's initiatives exemplify how infrastructure investments can enhance resilience in water supply systems.The urgency of the situation is underscored by the imminent expiration of current water management rules in the fall of 2026. As states grapple with the looming deadline to ratify Colorado River water allocation agreements, PCL Construction's projects, such as the Drought Pipeline Program in Phoenix, provide tangible examples of how communities can adapt to a drier future. This program has been crucial in ensuring the reliability of water supply for the rapidly growing Phoenix area, which has seen a 12% population increase over the last decade.Industry leaders, including PCL, recently gathered at the Colorado River Water Users Association Conference to discuss collaborative strategies for securing the river's future. The consensus was clear: a multifaceted approach is necessary to address the complexities of climate change and aging infrastructure. PCL Construction's commitment to advancing water reuse initiatives positions the company as a critical player in shaping sustainable water management practices that can withstand the challenges posed by the Colorado River's ongoing crisis.

The Western Kentucky University (WKU) Student Government Association (SGA) has officially announced the approval of WKU's public-private partnership with Gilbane. This significant development indicates the beginning of a collaborative effort aimed at enhancing student facilities and resources. As part of the partnership, which was formally approved by WKU’s Board of Regents on 11th December 2025, Gilbane will design and develop a new residence hall, intended to replace the outdated Douglas Keen and Hugh Poland Halls.Following the recent approval from the Kentucky Legislature on 6th April 2026, WKU is poised to commence construction on the new residence halls. SGA Senator Jakob Barker confirmed that the partnership with Gilbane is now fully authorized, stating, "WKU is now in the clear to start building the new residence halls." This development not only addresses housing needs but also reflects WKU's commitment to improving the overall student experience.In addition to the housing initiative, the SGA has passed several legislative measures aimed at enhancing student resources. Among these is Resolution 7-26-S, which supports the installation of a crosswalk at a crucial intersection near student housing. The resolution highlights the safety concerns of students who currently navigate busy streets to access essential facilities, advocating for collaboration with the Kentucky Department of Transportation for this project.Further addressing financial constraints faced by students, Bill 17-26-S was also approved, establishing the Textbooks for Toppers reimbursement program. This initiative will provide $50 reimbursements for students purchasing required course materials, a move that reflects the SGA's recognition of the financial challenges many students encounter. Additionally, other bills passed will provide essential academic supplies, such as Scantrons and lab goggles, ensuring that students have access to necessary materials without added financial burden.As WKU moves forward with its partnership with Gilbane and implements these new funding initiatives, the SGA's commitment to improving the student experience is clear. The collaboration with Gilbane not only aims to enhance student housing but also signifies a broader strategy to address various student needs and concerns, fostering a supportive academic environment.

+ GILBANE LAUNCHES LEASING FOR $135M AFFORDABLE HOUSING PROJECT IN D.C.

WASHINGTON, D.C. — Gilbane Development has begun leasing the second of its three-phase redevelopment at 7th Street and Barnaby Road in Southeast Washington, D.C. As of 3rd April 2026, Phase II of Barnaby & 7th includes 229 units within four new residential buildings: The Stoneview, The Solomon, The Marte, and The Lewis.Construction of these buildings is slated for completion by summer 2026. The four communities feature a mix of studio to three-bedroom units serving households earning 50 and 80 percent of the area median income (AMI). Notably, twenty percent of the apartments will be set aside as Permanent Supportive Housing (PSH) units, which will be managed by the district’s Department of Homeless Services.Phase I of Barnaby & 7th consisted of The Upland, which was completed in spring 2025 and includes 169 apartments, with 34 designated as PSH units. The income restrictions for this phase ranged from 30 to 50 percent of AMI. The third phase of development is expected to include approximately 100 additional rental units in one multifamily building, bringing the project to a grand total of 498 units upon completion.During the construction of Phase II, Gilbane also built a new pedestrian walkway designed to connect all six buildings of the larger development. According to the District of Columbia Housing Finance Agency, the total project cost for Phase II was approximately $135 million, underscoring Gilbane's commitment to affordable housing in the region.As the project progresses, it is anticipated to significantly contribute to the availability of affordable housing options in Washington, D.C., while also enhancing community connectivity and providing essential support services for residents in need.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLY REINER CACERES JOINS CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP AS HR DIRECTOR

Carly Reiner Caceres has been appointed as the Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction Group, bringing a wealth of experience in people operations and talent strategy. On the 29th of April 2026, Caceres officially began her new role, where she will be instrumental in supporting national campus recruiting initiatives aimed at bolstering early-career talent pipelines for one of the largest building and infrastructure companies in the United States.Prior to her appointment at Clark Construction Group, Caceres served as the Vice President of People Operations at Proper, where she was pivotal in shaping the HR strategy and operational support within a hybrid work environment. Her extensive background includes significant roles at MILLER as the Director of Internal Operations and more than nine years at Accenture, where she excelled in various capacities including Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Caceres’ career trajectory showcases her expertise in full-cycle recruiting, stakeholder management, and employer branding, which will be crucial as Clark Construction Group seeks to attract top talent from universities across the nation. Her previous experience managing campus recruiting strategies at Grant Thornton LLP further solidifies her capabilities in building robust internship programs and enhancing student engagement initiatives.Clark Construction Group, founded in 1906, has established a strong reputation for delivering value across major markets, including education and infrastructure. With a commitment to community strengthening and project excellence, the company is poised to benefit from Caceres' strategic insights and leadership in HR, particularly in nurturing the next generation of professionals in the construction industry.Caceres’ early career involved internships and roles with various organizations, including Charleston Magazine and the Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum, which helped her build a diverse skill set in communications and external relations. As she embarks on this new chapter at Clark Construction Group, her multifaceted background is expected to contribute significantly to the company's growth and talent development objectives.

+ CLARK CONTRACTS SECURES MULTI-TRADES FRAMEWORK WITH RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL

Clark Contracts has been appointed to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades contractors framework, enhancing the delivery of repair, maintenance, and refurbishment services to domestic properties across the local authority area. This announcement was made on 11th February 2026.The framework, which is set to run until 2027, encompasses a variety of multi-trade works. This will enable Renfrewshire Council to effectively manage both responsive and planned maintenance services across its housing stock, ensuring that local residents receive prompt and reliable service.Clark Contracts is already an established player in the public sector, holding several framework agreements that include Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework. Additionally, the company is involved with the Crown Commercial Service’s Construction Framework and various Scottish Procurement Alliance frameworks focusing on Public Buildings, New Build Housing, and more.Gordon Cunningham, managing director at Clark Contracts, expressed enthusiasm about the new appointment, saying, "Building on our strong track record of delivering more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over the past three years, we are pleased to have been appointed to Renfrewshire Council’s framework. As a Paisley-based business, we are proud to be supporting the council and delivering refurbishment and maintenance services that directly benefit local communities."The addition of this framework is expected to bolster Clark Contracts' already significant presence in the public sector, as the company continues to collaborate with various councils and institutions, including the University of Glasgow and Glasgow City Council, to deliver essential services.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP ADVANCES ₱4-B MIXED-USE HUB AT THE VILLAGES

CLARK FREEPORT – A ₱4-billion mixed-use residential and commercial development in The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination here, has been given the green light with the approval by the Clark Development Corp. of an amendment to PKR Corp.’s lease agreement on 13th December 2025. This ambitious project underscores Clark Construction Group's aggressive strategy to attract significant investments and fortify its reputation as Asia’s Leading Meetings and Conference Destination, a title recently bestowed by the World Travel Awards.The revised lease agreement provides PKR Corp. with a 30-month timeline to complete construction and incorporates stringent performance safeguards to ensure timely project delivery. Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera, president and CEO of Clark Development Corp. (CDC), emphasized that the amendment reflects the organization's commitment to fostering investor confidence and efficient execution of projects. "With this agreement, we ensure that it is a good place for everyone to go and with our coordination, we want to be able to maintain that area [The Villages], an area of preference, where people can reside and really be part of the community," Devanadera stated.PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung expressed gratitude for the partnership with CDC, stating, "We are honored to take part in Clark’s growth and with CDC as our partner, we will deliver this development in Clark." PKR Corp. operates under the umbrella of Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean firm known for its expertise in building, civil, residential, and infrastructure projects. The company has received accolades for safety, technology, and workplace practices from various esteemed organizations including the Korea Expressway Corp. and the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy.This mixed-use development is part of CDC’s broader strategy to expedite investments, promote well-planned communities, and establish a business-friendly environment that appeals to both global and local investors. The focus on creating integrated lifestyle hubs aligns with the increasing demand for residential options that complement commercial and recreational spaces, positioning Clark as an attractive destination for future developments.Clark Construction Group's proactive approach in securing this project represents a significant step forward in the region's economic landscape, potentially catalyzing further investments and developments. As the construction timeline unfolds, stakeholders will be keenly observing how this initiative contributes to the overall growth and vibrancy of The Villages and the Clark Freeport Zone.

value.Title: Indra Energy 's New, Renewable-Backed Plans Signal a Shift In How Homeowners Choose Clean Power

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJG-N3N3-RSVB-V2S9-00000-00Indra Energy's New, Renewable-Backed Plans Signal a Shift In How Homeowners Choose Clean Power2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:45:02ZLexisNexisRENEWABLE ENERGY NEWSIndra Energy's New, Renewable-Backed Plans Signal a Shift In How Homeowners Choose Clean Power

Houston, TX, United States, May 11th, 2026, FinanceWire

Indra Energy, a provider of renewable energy plans, has announced an expanded focus on improving access to renewable energy for homeowners amid evolving market conditions. The development comes as deregulated energy markets and increasing demand for clean energy continue to influence how consumers source electricity.

The company stated that it is positioning its renewable-backed energy plans to address barriers to entry that have historically limited homeowner participation in renewable energy programs. By leveraging existing market structures, Indra Energy aims to offer alternatives that may broaden access without requiring significant infrastructure changes at the household level.

In a recent interview, Jeff Rodgers, Chief Marketing Officer at Indra Energy, discussed the company's approach to making renewable energy options more widely available. He outlined how the company's plan offerings are designed to align with changing consumer preferences and regulatory environments.

The Power of Choice

For many years, the majority of homeowners haven't really had a choice about where they get their power. In many cities and states across the country, customers are restricted to a single electricity supplier that has an essential monopoly over this service. For homeowners who want to switch to "clean" power sources, installing rooftop solar panels is the only option.

Entering energy deregulation - a system that has seen growing adoption across the country to give homeowners the ability to choose their energy supplier. Unlike areas that have a regulated monopoly, deregulated markets allow a variety of suppliers to compete in a particular market. According to Rodgers, market deregulation is an important first step in expanding clean energy access.

"With deregulated energy markets, homeowners are in control of choosing their power supplier - and that includes having the option to use clean energy providers," he says. "Deregulated markets tend to lead the way in clean energy adoption simply because there isn't a utility monopoly that controls everything. When people have the ability to choose, those who prioritize clean energy are going to make that a priority in their supplier search."

To that end, Indra Energy has begun introducing plans for electricity and natural gas in parts of the Northeast and Midwest U.S. that offer deregulated markets. Residents of Delaware, Indiana, Massachusetts, Michigan, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, Virginia and the District of Columbia can receive electricity, natural gas or both utilities through Indra Energy.

No Solar Panels Required

Another long-standing belief regarding clean power is that it requires homeowners to install solar panels or other equipment to generate renewable energy on their own. While solar panels are growing in popularity, they are often inaccessible. A report by the National Renewable Energy Laboratory determined that 49% of households in the United States could not install a solar panel system because of insufficient roof space, insufficient roof access (such as in a multi-unit housing facility) or renting status.

Aside from these broad concerns, many homeowners simply can't afford to install solar panels because of financial constraints, while local building codes restricting panel placement can also limit energy generation capacity.

Rodgers and the Indra Energy team have opted for a simpler approach that streamlines clean energy access for homeowners. "Our electricity plans are backed by Renewable Energy Credits (RECs). These are issued to certify when one megawatt-hour of electricity has been generated by a renewable source like solar, wind or hydro," he explains."For our electric plans, Indra Energy purchases energy from RECs for the individual homeowners' energy usage. Similarly, our natural gas plans are paired with carbon offsets. We match natural gas use with verified offset projects designed to reduce or remove greenhouse gases so that you will have net-zero emissions from your energy use."Rodgers notes that this setup means homeowners aren't necessarily getting "green electrons" delivered directly to their home. "As an energy supplier, when we purchase RECs, it ensures that renewable energy is added to the grid in an amount that equals your usage. The electricity delivered to your home comes from the grid mix, which can include a mix of renewable and non-renewable sources. While you may not be directly getting renewables, you can have confidence that your usage has been matched with renewable energy, so you're still having a positive impact on the environment."

A Streamlined Way to Switch

With no additional equipment required, the process of switching to Indra Energy is surprisingly simple. When homeowners in deregulated markets select Indra Energy as their supplier, their electricity and natural gas continue to be delivered through existing utility lines.

While the supplier changes, the local utility still handles ongoing service, including system maintenance and repairs, meter reads and billing. Because energy is provided through existing lines, homeowners don't experience any interruption to their service.

"I hope this really changes the way people think about accessing renewable energy," Rodgers says. "It doesn't have to be this big expensive process that might not even work for your property. It can be as simple as signing up for a new energy provider and having access to clean energy, without any visible change to your home or daily routine. When accessibility becomes the standard, green energy adoption can increase dramatically and we can have a bigger collective impact on the environment."

As Rodgers and Indra Energy illustrate, how homeowners are accessing clean energy is rapidly changing. With more accessible solutions readily available, green energy is poised to expand beyond its relatively niche adoption rates into something that can be used by a broader range of homeowners than ever before.

About Indra Energy

Indra Energy is an independent, licensed supplier of renewable electricity and carbon-offset natural gas. Its residential energy plans help customers reduce their carbon footprint without installing solar panels by supplying electricity backed by Renewable Energy Certificates (RECs) and offsetting all natural gas usage with carbon credits. Indra Energy currently offers plans across seven markets and 39 utilities in Delaware, Massachusetts, Michigan, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, Virginia and the District of Columbia.

Contact

Indra Energy

hello@indraenergy.com

urn:contentItem:6JHJ-02F3-S13P-437N-00000-00Interim Report Analysis Half Year ending 31 March 2026: Cabot EPS drops 22.1%2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:58ZLexisNexisInterim Report Analysis Half Year ending 31 March 2026: Cabot EPS drops 22.1%

AMERICAN RESULTS

Cabot (NYSE:CBT) announced EPS of $2.65 for the half year-ended 31 March 2026, down 22.1% from $3.4 in the previous corresponding period.

Half-Yearly Report (31 Mar 2026)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; 31 Mar 2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] Revenue was down 7.3% and Net Profit was down 24.6%.

Half year-ended31 Mar 202631 Mar 2025EPS, $2.63.4PCP growth in EPS %-22.142.3Revenue, $ billion1.81.9PCP growth in Revenue %-7.3-4.4Net Profit, $ million141187PCP growth in Net Profit %-24.639.6

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Unfavourable Changes:

- Net profit slumped 24.6% from $187.0m to $141.0m

- Total revenue was down 7.3% from $1.9b to $1.8b

- EPS slumped 22.1% from $3.4 to $2.65

- EBIT to Revenues was down from 15.6% to 13.6%

- Profit before tax to Revenues was down from 15.6% to 13.6%

- Profit after tax to Revenues was down from 9.9% to 8%

Half-Yearly Report (31 Mar 2026)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; 31 Mar 2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] Revenue was down 7.3% and Net Profit was down 24.6%.

ACTIVITIES:

Cabot Corporation is a global leader in specialty chemicals and performance materials, headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts, and listed on the New York Stock Exchange. The company's core business activities focus on the production and supply of advanced materials such as carbon black, which is critical for tires, industrial rubber, and plastics; fumed silica for adhesives, coatings, and electronics; and specialty additives for energy storage and inkjet applications. These offerings serve diverse industries including automotive, construction, and electronics, emphasizing innovation in sustainable and high-performance solutions. The fastest-growing segments include battery materials and sustainable products, fueled by rising demand for electric vehicles, renewable energy technologies, and eco-friendly alternatives. With a commitment to research and development, Cabot maintains a strong global footprint, operating in over 20 countries and generating revenue through strategic partnerships and operational efficiency.

MARKET POSITION:

Cabot Corporation (NYSE:CBT), the NYSE's 19th largest Chemicals company by market cap, increased $3.31 (or 4.4%) in the trailing 12 months to May 05, 2026 to close at $78.93. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 27.6% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -23.3%. Average daily volume of 428,362 shares in the trailing year was 1.1 times average daily volume in the previous year.

value.Title: Isabella Weber: "Raising interest rates is economically ill-advised and politically risky"

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGR-KHJ3-S68D-814K-00000-00Isabella Weber: "Raising interest rates is economically ill-advised and politically risky"2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:57ZLexisNexisIsabella Weber: "Raising interest rates is economically ill-advised and politically risky"


Isabella Weber (Nuremberg, Germany, 39) became one of the leading critics of the policies adopted by central banks during the 2022 energy crisis. Amid her hectic schedule during the Global Progressive Mobilization, Weber takes time to find a secluded office within the Fira de Barcelona complex. The German, a professor at the University of Massachusetts Amherst, believes that Europe must resume its plans to roll out renewable energy if it wants to overcome the current economic, political, and climate crisis.


Question. We had forgotten about inflation, but geopolitical tensions have brought it back. Have we moved from a deflationary decade to a new era in which we will have to live with more frequent price hikes?
Answer. We are in an era of overlapping crises; the global order is disintegrating, and climate change is a reality. In this environment, the question was not whether there would be other shocks, but when and where the next one would occur. I wish we hadn't had that war in Iran. But yes, we are in a new inflationary environment that is an expression of economic, political, and ecological instability. It stems neither from monetary policy nor from excessive public spending, but rather from the profound crisis we are facing.
Q. Analysts are already expecting several interest rate hikes from the European Central Bank. Do you think Frankfurt should act now?
A. The ECB should not raise rates. If it does, it will make the investments we urgently need to increase the resilience of our economies more expensive. But what we are experiencing is also a crisis of democracy. And the logic of raising interest rates and imposing austerity on countries that are going to go into debt to cover increased defense spending is extremely dangerous politically. Rate hikes aim to cool the labor market but end up creating unemployment and preventing wages from keeping pace with inflation. This increases economic insecurity, which is one of the main drivers of support for the far right around the world. If interest rates are used now, it will be an inappropriate tool from an economic standpoint and a dangerous one from a political perspective.
Q. You were very critical of the ECB's actions during the last crisis, but in the end, inflation did go down. Do you still think those rate hikes were a mistake?
A. I'm not saying we should have negative or near-zero interest rates, as happened at one point. We want rates that are somewhat higher so as not to fuel asset price bubbles and highly speculative dynamics. But I don't believe inflation subsided because of the ECB's actions, but rather because those shocks hit the economy.
Q. You also warned that companies contributed to the inflationary spiral to protect their margins.
A. Yes, corporations did not absorb the shocks; instead, they passed them on to consumers to protect their margins. In some cases, they even raised them, and that was one of the main drivers of inflation. The energy crisis created a window where companies knew they could raise prices without risking losing market share because they were certain that competitors would do the same. It was an implicit coordination that allowed them to exercise their market power in a way they couldn't before the crisis.
Q. In a crisis, there are always winners and losers. Who came out on top?
A. In the United States, profit margins in the non-financial sector skyrocketed in a way we had practically never seen before. To get [former U.S. President] Joe Biden to exempt them from a windfall profits tax, companies argued that their shareholders were the American people, and that if they did well, so would the citizens. But we've investigated this and found that the top 1% captured 50% of the profits, while the bottom 50% managed to capture only 1% of the profits. This shows us that these kinds of profit spikes are fundamentally unequal.
Q. Brussels has issued its recommendations in the early stages of this crisis, and member states have begun to adopt measures. Do we need to act faster?
A. Without a doubt. We're already seeing a pretty big fire. Even if the war ended tomorrow, we'd have a massive energy crisis. Analysts are already talking about jet fuel shortages, and a shortage of other fossil fuels could follow very soon. It is time for the European Union to act. The most obvious step is to revive measures from the last crisis: the emergency gasoline price cap that never took effect, or making public transportation free. But there is also a broader allocation problem.
Q. What do you propose?
A. Poorer countries, which are just as or even more dependent on imports, will literally run out of oil or be left with very little supply. This means that poor people, who already consume less energy than the rich, would have to make greater savings. But they can't: they already take the bus or walk to work. Their carbon footprint is so small that saving energy is inhumane for them. That leads me to propose a multilateral buyers' club led by Europe.
Q. But is Europe in a position to lead it?
A. Europe accounts for 21% of global oil imports. That would likely not be enough. It would need to invite other high-income countries to join. Not all would do so, but if possible, it should partner with Japan, Korea, Singapore, Canada, or Australia and then invite low-income countries to join, as they would have every incentive in the world to do so. It may sound utopian, but the looming crisis is massive, so we must think innovatively. And it would be an opportunity for Europe to position itself as an independent power capable of multilateral cooperation, rather than letting the United States trigger a global economic collapse with this war. It is also a geopolitical issue.
Q. And how would it be distributed among the club's members?
A. Low-income countries, collectively, account for only 0.1% of imports. All countries should be allowed to consume the equivalent of what they had before. The rest should commit to a portion of what they consumed before the crisis, which should be negotiated. That would ensure everyone saves equally. In other words, if there is a 25% shortage in the global market, everyone should save 25%. It's another form of rationing: in one case, it's driven by skyrocketing prices; in this case, by a multilateral agreement.
Q. The outbreak of the war in Ukraine put an end to the European economic model based on imports of cheap Russian gas. Is the EU in a better position today than it was in 2022?
A. There has been investment, but not enough. Spain did invest more than any other European country. If other countries had followed Spain's example, we would likely be in a less concerning situation. We needed a massive push, a true mobilization to activate all available resources to boost renewables. And that hasn't happened.
Q. The energy sector claims that the windfall profits tax is stifling investment in renewables. Is there any truth to that?
A. In 2022, the fossil fuel industry's profits exceeded all renewable energy investments combined in the United States. And that year, they also saw a significant boost in stock market valuations.
Q. Has the EU allowed the Green Deal to languish over the years?
A. Yes, and it's quite dystopian. We need it to address the climate crisis, but also the geopolitical crisis, to stop depending on gas from Russia or the United States. How can we expect our economies to depend on either of those two powers? From a sovereignty perspective, the path of renewables is the only one we can take. But the European Commission isn't doing it.
Q. At the progressive congress, you emphasize the crisis of democracy. What does that entail?
A. It's great to be able to vote and participate. But democratic representatives then say they can't do anything when it comes to your most basic material needs, such as the cost of energy, food, transportation, or housing. At that point, the procedural merits of democracy ring hollow, even more so in a crisis like the one we're facing, where shocks are systemic expressions of the crisis. If governments allow speculation to generate profits that end up redistributing wealth from the bottom up while people are struggling to make ends meet, we are facing a failure of democracy. States must also act as guarantors of people's ability to live with dignity.

urn:contentItem:6JGC-03G3-RS3Y-F0KC-00000-00Researchers from Harvard University Report Recent Findings in Sustainable Development (Comparative PEST analysis for sustainable development of the electricity market in Kazakhstan)2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:58ZLexisNexisSUSTAINABILITY RESEARCH - SUSTAINABLE DEVELOPMENTResearchers from Harvard University Report Recent Findings in Sustainable Development (Comparative PEST analysis for sustainable development of the electricity market in Kazakhstan)


2026 APR 01 (NewsRx) -- By a News Reporter-Staff News Editor at Ivy League Daily News -- Investigators discuss new findings in sustainable development. According to news originating from Harvard University by NewsRx editors, the research stated, "As Kazakhstan confronts the imperative of energy transition, this study elucidates strategic pathways to fortify its energy market through phased renewable integration and fair policy to support green energy. Kazakhstan's energy sector is characterized by a heavy reliance on hydrocarbons, particularly coal, which has significant implications for greenhouse gas emissions and air pollution."


Financial supporters for this research include Ministry of Science And Higher Education of The Republic of Kazakhstan.
Our news correspondents obtained a quote from the research from Harvard University: "Despite efforts to transition to clean technologies, the country faces numerous challenges. This study employs PEST analysis to systematically evaluate the political, economic, social, and technological factors impacting the adoption of renewable energy in Kazakhstan. The research focuses on external macro-environmental factors and contextualizes findings within recent geopolitical and economic developments. This study identifies best practices from energy markets of leading global players (the European Union, the United States, and China) and evaluates their applicability to Kazakhstan. The study highlights the imperative for legislative enhancements, infrastructure modernization, and financial and insurance reforms to foster investment and stability in the sector. It also underscores the necessity of public-private partnerships and educational initiatives to boost societal engagement and support. Technological innovation is pinpointed as crucial for optimizing energy management and advancing toward carbon neutrality."
According to the news reporters, the research concluded: "The findings propose actionable policy recommendations to reduce Kazakhstan's reliance on fossil fuels, increasing resource efficiency, and achieving sustainable energy development. This research aims to contribute to a deeper understanding of the barriers specific to the renewable energy market in Kazakhstan and inform policy decisions that promote a low-carbon transition."
For more information on this research see: Comparative PEST analysis for sustainable development of the electricity market in Kazakhstan. Heliyon, 2026,12(7):e44846. (Heliyon - http://www.heliyon.com). The publisher for Heliyon is Elsevier.
A free version of this journal article is available at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.heliyon.2026.e44846.
Our news editors report that more information may be obtained by contacting Nurkhat Zhakiyev, Davis Center for Russian and Eurasian Studies, Harvard University, Cambridge, MA, 02138, United States. Additional authors for this research include Ayagoz Khamzina, Sivapriya Mothilal Bhagavathy, Leila Salykova, Samal Zakaryanova, Naziya Baisakalova.
Keywords for this news article include: Harvard University, Energy, Oil & Gas, Renewable Energy, Sustainability Research, Sustainable Development.
Our reports deliver fact-based news of research and discoveries from around the world. Copyright 2026, NewsRx LLC

value.Title: Trump’s Renewable Energy Crackdown Hits Legal Wall

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH0-BP13-S0PB-7364-00000-00Trump’s Renewable Energy Crackdown Hits Legal Wall2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:57ZLexisNexisTrump’s Renewable Energy Crackdown Hits Legal Walladmin

By Felicity Bradstock

A federal judge ruled that requiring personal approval for all wind and solar projects was unlawful and harmful to public interests.

The policy had already delayed or threatened over 57 GW of renewable capacity and billions in investment.

The decision marks the latest in a string of court rulings pushing back against federal attempts to curb clean energy growth.

Over the last year, U.S. President Donald Trump has taken aim at the renewable energy industry, in an attempt to scale back efforts to undergo a green transition. Trump has instead favoured the expansion of the oil, gas, and coal industries, as well as the development of nuclear power. However, the move has been met with significant public and legal pushback, as it becomes clear that halting the rollout of renewable energy will not be so simple.

In April, federal judge Denise J Casper in Massachusetts rejected the Trump administration’s efforts to halt renewable energy deployment. The judge ruled that the requirement for the Interior Secretary, Doug Burgum, to personally approve all solar and wind energy projects on federal lands and waters was unlawful. Casper agreed that the Trump administration’s actions likely violate federal statute and would cause irreparable harm if the court did not intervene.

The Trump administration previously said that greater oversight was required for new projects to counteract the preferential treatment of renewable energy that was seen under the Biden administration. It gave Burgum permission to review and veto issues such as proposed leases to rights of way, construction and operational plans, grants, and biological opinions. As part of the ruling, Casper issued a preliminary injunction to prevent the government from implementing this policy.

The lawsuit was launched in December by a coalition of regional wind and solar developers, including the Alliance for Clean Energy New York, MAREC Action, Southern Renewable Energy Association, Clean Grid Alliance, Interwest Energy Alliance, Renewable Northwest, Carolinas Clean Energy Business Association, Renew Northeast and Green Energy Consumers Alliance.

The coalition feared the policy could threaten the development of solar and wind projects. The members argued that Burgum favoured fossil fuels and aimed to delay renewable energy development to enhance the position of oil and gas. The lawsuit challenged six final agency actions that relegated wind and solar technologies to “second-class status”.

The challenged actions include:

The DOI

“review procedures” memo

, which made 68 specific permitting actions subject to review by Burgum;

A ban on the use of the Information for Planning and Consultation website

by solar and wind developers before their projects are reviewed;

The DOI “

land order

,” which significantly restricted the development of wind and solar projects on federal land.

An

Army Corps’ memo

that instructs the agency to prioritise its permit reviews for high-capacity density projects; and

The

Zerzan M-opinion

, which essentially bans new offshore wind projects.

The Trump administration’s attack on renewable energy over the last year has resulted in the delay or cancellation of approximately 57.2 GW of wind, solar, hybrid, and offshore wind capacity across projects with around $905 million in capital invested. The government’s renewable energy restrictions put between $8.4 billion and $25.6 billion of federal tax credits for renewable energy development at risk over the next three years, according to Judge Casper.

Casper stated, “Plaintiffs have shown that the public interest favours preliminary relief from the Agency Actions because the Agency Actions ‘harm the public by delaying and preventing the development of wind and solar energy projects in the United States, which in turn threatens the public’s vital interest in maintaining a reliable, affordable, and resilient power grid, which is currently struggling to meet record energy demand.’”

The plaintiffs said that the ruling marked a positive step towards developing more affordable energy options for U.S. consumers. “Clean energy is fast, affordable and here to stay… We look forward to getting back to work and restarting the impacted wind and solar projects nationwide,” the statement said.

Meanwhile, the managing director for power at the Natural Resources Defence Council, Kit Kennedy, stated, “The administration should take the hint and stop these illegal attacks on projects that will help meet surging electricity demand and bring down costs for consumers.”

The ruling comes just three months after a court ruled in favour of the ongoing development of offshore wind projects. In January, a U.S. federal court granted a preliminary injunction permitting the construction of the Revolution Wind offshore wind project to restart. This move temporarily blocked suspension orders issued by the Bureau of Ocean Energy Management in late 2025.

Then, in February, a federal judge threw out the Interior Department’s halt work order on a multibillion-dollar wind farm off the coast of New York State, marking the fifth time that U.S. courts ruled against Trump administration efforts to stop offshore wind development.

However, Trump has previously been known to throw money at the problem to make it go away. Following the court rulings in favour of offshore wind development, the Trump administration offered France’s TotalEnergies almost $1 billion to permanently halt its wind projects. This makes one wonder how Trump will respond to the latest ruling on his administration’s policies attacking renewable energy development. While the ruling could help provide temporary relief for green energy developers, the Trump administration is unlikely to back down on its efforts to quash green energy development.

value.Title: Primoris Services Investors Should Contact Block &Leviton to Possibly Recover Losses

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJG-HP53-S45J-X1HK-00000-00Primoris Services Investors Should Contact Block &Leviton to Possibly Recover Losses2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:57ZLexisNexisPrimoris Services Investors Should Contact Block &Leviton to Possibly Recover Losses

PRIMORIS SERVICES CORP ("PRIM-N") - Primoris Services Investors Should Contact Block &Leviton - to Possibly Recover Losses Block &Leviton is investigating Primoris Services Corporation for potential securities law violations. Investors who have lost money in their Primoris Services Corporation investment should contact the firm to learn more about how they might recover those losses. For more details, visit https://blockleviton.com/cases/prim. What is this all about?

Primoris Services Corporation's stock fell over 40% in intraday trading on May 6, 2026, after the company reported Q1 2026 results below analyst expectations and slashed full-year adjusted EBITDA guidance from $560-$580 million to $480-$500 million. In its May 5, 2026 earnings release, Primoris attributed the reduction to lower renewable energy activity, delayed project starts, and increased costs on renewable energy projects. This contrasts with statements made on the company's February 24, 2026 Q4 2025 earnings call, when Primoris told investors, "We've accounted for all of these increased costs and expect renewables margins to improve as we progress into 2026." Who is eligible? Anyone who purchased Primoris Services Corporation common stock and has seen their shares fall may be eligible, whether or not they have sold their investment. Investors should contact Block &Leviton to learn more. What is Block &Leviton doing? Block &Leviton is investigating whether the Company committed securities law violations and may file an action to attempt to recover losses on behalf of investors who have lost money. What should you do next? If you've lost money on your investment, you should contact Block &Leviton to learn more via our case website, by email at shareholders@blockleviton.com, or by phone at (888) 256-2510. Whistleblower? If you have non-public information about Primoris Services Corporation, you should consider assisting in our investigation or working with our attorneys to file a report with the Securities Exchange Commission under their whistleblower program. Whistleblowers who provide original information to the SEC may receive rewards of up to 30% of any successful recovery. For more information, contact Block &Leviton at shareholders@blockleviton.com or by phone at (888) 256-2510. Why should you contact Block &Leviton? Block &Leviton is widely regarded as one of the leading securities class action firms in the country. Our attorneys have recovered billions of dollars for defrauded investors and are dedicated to obtaining significant recoveries on behalf of our clients through active litigation in the federal courts across the country. Many of the nation's top institutional investors hire us to represent their interests. You can learn more about us at our website www.blockleviton.com, call (888) 256-2510 or email shareholders@blockleviton.com with any questions. This notice may constitute attorney advertising. CONTACT: BLOCK &LEVITON LLP 260 Franklin St., Suite 1860 Boston, MA 02110 Phone: (888) 256-2510 Email: shareholders@blockleviton.com

value.Title: May 04, 2026: FAEGRE DRINKER BIDDLE & REATH LLP AND PEERS INDUSTRY NEWS COMPILATION

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHB-59C3-S8C5-12Y1-00000-00May 04, 2026: FAEGRE DRINKER BIDDLE & REATH LLP AND PEERS INDUSTRY NEWS COMPILATION2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:57ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 04, 2026: FAEGRE DRINKER BIDDLE & REATH LLP AND PEERS INDUSTRY NEWS COMPILATION

Faegre Drinker Biddle & Reath LLP recently expanded its strategic partnership with Opensity Solutions to enhance operational capabilities and client services. Effective April 2, 2026, the collaboration encompasses 17 offices nationwide, integrating essential services such as office management and information governance. John Stanley, Chief Administrative Officer of Faegre Drinker, stated that the firm is focused on building a modern, resilient operating model aligned with client needs. Additionally, Faegre Drinker bolstered its London finance and restructuring group with the hire of partner Eleni Zodiates, who brings extensive experience in corporate and real estate finance. The firm's intellectual property practice also earned significant recognition on January 30, 2026, securing top rankings across multiple regions in the World Trademark Review 1000.

In related industry developments, Winston & Strawn LLP announced the addition of Aimee Fagan as a partner in its Dallas Intellectual Property Practice, effective April 20, 2026. Fagan noted her excitement to join a team highly regarded for managing complex IP disputes. Winston & Strawn also highlighted a remarkable internal event on April 14, 2026, when it was shared that Partner Erik Harvey successfully received a kidney transplant from Associate Attorney Elizabeth Lawson in August 2025. Furthermore, the firm welcomed back Ashley Chung as a partner in its Appellate & Critical Motions practice in Chicago on April 6, 2026.

ReedSmith LLP announced strategic leadership changes, appointing a new leader for its Philadelphia office effective April 27, 2026, and naming its first Managing Partner in Boston starting April 23, 2026. The firm also strengthened its Miami office with a prominent fund finance professional acquired from a rival firm on April 17, 2026.

Jones Day saw Partner Anna Dwyer recognized as a Rising Star for the 2026 Women of Influence Awards on April 21, 2026, for her extensive commercial real estate practice. The firm also added Paul Stancil as Of Counsel in its Business & Tort Litigation Practice in Dallas on April 10, 2026.

Greenberg Traurig, LLP reported that its Environmental and Real Estate practices were shortlisted for the 2026 Chambers Law Firm of the Year awards on April 23, 2026. On the same date, the firm's London Real Estate Finance team announced it had advised on transactions exceeding £5 billion.

Dentons elevated four partners in its UKIME region, effective May 1, 2026. The firm also facilitated AD Ports Group's warehouse sale to Aldar for $177 million on April 27, 2026, and advised Global Relay on a major London office acquisition on April 23, 2026.

Norton Rose Fulbright and Latham & Watkins successfully advised on a $2.2 billion renewable energy joint venture between Masdar and TotalEnergies, announced on April 21, 2026. On that same day, Norton Rose Fulbright acted as the legal adviser for Diafa in the £1.4 billion acquisition of the Ivy Collection.

Morgan Lewis & Bockius LLP provided legal guidance for Lime Rock New Energy, which closed its second fund with $640 million in commitments on April 22, 2026. The firm also represented Quintillion in a strategic acquisition by GCI Holdings LLC announced on April 28, 2026.

Foley & Lardner LLP relocated its Jacksonville office to a newly developed downtown space on March 25, 2026. The firm also provided legal commentary on the Securities and Exchange Commission's updated Enforcement Manual, which was modified on February 27, 2026.

Holland & Knight experienced personnel shifts as well, with Bracewell expanding its Dallas real estate team by hiring two professionals from the firm on March 9, 2026. Coinciding with this date, Holland & Knight appointed a new mergers and acquisitions specialist to lead its Tampa operations.
