urn:contentItem:6JHT-DBX3-RXH7-24F5-00000-00SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION - SECTOR UPDATES FROM PEERS2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:57ZLexisNexisSTOCKSUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION - SECTOR UPDATES FROM PEERS

DASHBOARD: SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION

PermID5000317077Websitehttp://www.suffolk.com/IndustryConstruction & Engineering

ACTIVITIES:

Suffolk Construction, founded in 1982 by John Fish in Boston, Massachusetts, is a leading U.S. general contractor. With $5.5 billion in revenue and 2,500 employees, Suffolk delivers complex projects in healthcare, education, and commercial sectors. Notable works include Bostons Millennium Tower and Miamis Brickell City Centre. Employing advanced technologies like BIM and virtual design, Suffolk enhances project efficiency and safety. The company operates 10 offices nationwide, with a strong Northeast presence. Suffolks commitment to sustainability includes LEED-certified projects and eco-friendly practices. Its employee-centric culture, with robust training and diversity initiatives, earned it a spot on Fortunes Best Workplaces list. Suffolk continues to innovate, focusing on smart buildings and urban development.

+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION'S LANE REOPENING ON ROUTE 58 SIGNALS PROGRESS

SUFFOLK, Va. — Motorists on U.S. Route 58 near downtown Suffolk will soon see relief as the Virginia Department of Transportation (VDOT) begins removing a long-term lane closure starting as early as Monday night. As of 5th May 2026, the right lane closure on Route 58 westbound just east of the downtown exit will be reopened, allowing all three lanes of westbound traffic to flow freely once again.The lane closure has been in effect since March, coinciding with the ongoing Southeastern Public Service Authority interchange project managed by Suffolk Construction, which began in August 2024. The completion of this project aims to facilitate a new ramp and flyover for eastbound traffic, enabling vehicles to access the regional landfill without crossing over westbound lanes, thereby improving traffic safety and efficiency.While the reopening of the lane is a welcome change, motorists should be aware that some temporary overnight closures may occur as crews work to remove the temporary barriers. These closures will take place from 8 p.m. to 5 a.m., depending on weather conditions and scheduling factors. Long-term shoulder closures remain in effect in both directions, and intermittent lane closures or brief stoppages may still be possible as the project progresses.The speed limit through this construction zone is set at 50 miles per hour, and speed ticket cameras are actively monitoring both directions to ensure compliance. Motorists are encouraged to stay informed about the ongoing work and any potential disruptions by visiting VDOT's dedicated project website.As Suffolk Construction continues to make strides on this critical infrastructure project, the reopening of Route 58 is a significant milestone that reflects the company's commitment to enhancing transportation in the region. Further updates regarding the project will be available as work continues.

+ VMO2 SECURES 10-YEAR SOLAR ENERGY PACT WITH SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION

Virgin Media O2 (VMO2) is taking a significant step towards enhancing its renewable energy footprint by entering into a 10-year Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with solar power provider Egg Power. This strategic collaboration will allow VMO2 to source energy from Egg's 70MW solar farm located in Suffolk, which is under construction and anticipated to commence power generation in 2027.The agreement, announced on 1st May 2026, is set to supply approximately 5% of VMO2's total energy demands, marking a noteworthy addition to the company's sustainability initiatives. Egg Power, as a natural energy partner for VMO2, is particularly aligned with the company’s goals, given that both entities are under the ownership of Liberty Global. This partnership is expected to significantly aid VMO2 in its pursuit of achieving Net Zero carbon emissions by 2040.Mark Hardman, Director of Finance Operations at VMO2, emphasized the importance of this agreement, stating, “This agreement with Egg Power is the latest step in Virgin Media O2’s journey to achieve net zero emissions by the end of 2040. We’re committed to growing and operating our business in a way that’s good for people and the planet, where we’re cutting carbon, securing renewable energy on a long-term basis, and sourcing renewable energy generation from the UK.”This new PPA with Egg Power complements a previous 10-year agreement VMO2 entered into for wind power with The Renewables Infrastructure Group last year. Together, these two agreements will enable VMO2 to source around 20% of its energy from renewable sources through Power Purchase Agreements, reinforcing the company's commitment to sustainability.As VMO2 continues to advance its renewable energy strategy, this latest partnership not only addresses immediate energy needs but also aligns with broader environmental goals. The growing reliance on renewable energy sources is becoming increasingly critical for companies like VMO2 as they navigate the challenges of climate change and strive for sustainable operational practices.

+ SUFFOLK CONSTRUCTION BRACES FOR $897M CAPITAL SPENDING PROPOSAL

Suffolk County Executive Edward P. Romaine has unveiled a capital budget proposal totaling $897 million for 2027, which aims to address critical infrastructure needs within Suffolk County. The proposal, revealed on 24th April 2026, is part of a broader three-year capital program that includes approximately $3 billion in investments, maintaining a similar figure to a previously adopted plan.This proposed budget marks a 15% increase compared to the adopted 2026 capital budget, emphasizing long-term public works projects. Romaine highlighted that the budget focuses on "infrastructure needs that have been neglected for so long," framing it as an investment in Suffolk County's future. The capital budget operates independently from the operating budget, which is anticipated in the fall.Among the key components of the 2027 budget are $208 million allocated for sewer upgrades, $200 million dedicated to a new transportation hub near the Ronkonkoma Long Island Rail Road station, and $128 million for various road, bridge, sidewalk, and public transit improvements. A significant aspect of the proposal is the $185 million earmarked for a new medical examiner building, which will feature advanced ventilation, security, and lab facilities. The current building, dating back to the late 1980s, has been deemed beyond repair.Construction on the new medical examiner facility is set to commence in 2027 following a $20 million bond authorized in February 2025 for design and engineering costs. The project is projected to be completed by 2029. Additionally, the budget outlines plans for the new Smith Point Bridge, which will enhance connectivity between Shirley and Fire Island, funded primarily through federal aid.In introducing the budget, Romaine, a Republican in his third year as County Executive, stressed its role in supporting farmland preservation, expanding recreational assets, and promoting growth in key economic areas. Presiding Officer Anthony Piccirillo has indicated that a working group will be established to review the proposed budget, with meetings scheduled to start in May following an analysis by the legislature's budget review office.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BALFOUR BEATTY [3 News That Matters]

+ BALFOUR BEATTY ELEVATES TRENT JOHNSON TO LEAD AVIATION OPERATIONS IN THE CAROLINAS

Balfour Beatty has promoted Trent Johnson to Vice President of operations in the Carolinas, effective immediately. Johnson's new role will see him leading and expanding the company’s aviation sector across North and South Carolina, building on a career that spans over 19 years in the construction industry.As of 1st April 2026, Trent Johnson’s promotion is a reflection of his extensive experience, including more than 12 and a half years with Balfour Beatty. During his tenure, he has successfully contributed to the delivery of 22 aviation projects, ranging from targeted renovations to complex, multi-phase programmes. His leadership has been pivotal in enhancing Balfour Beatty’s aviation presence, notably at Raleigh-Durham International Airport (RDU) and Albert J. Ellis Airport.In his new capacity, Johnson will continue to support Balfour Beatty’s existing aviation clients while expanding the company’s footprint at additional regional and international airports throughout the Carolinas. His focus will remain on assisting project teams in navigating the complexities of active airport environments, while prioritizing safety, operational continuity, and stakeholder coordination.Jeff Sandeen, President of Balfour Beatty in the Carolinas, commented on Johnson’s promotion, stating, “Trent has built a strong reputation as a trusted leader and client partner within our local aviation operations. His ability to guide teams through the complexities of active airport environments has been a key driver of our success in the region.” Sandeen expressed confidence that Johnson will play a crucial role in shaping the next phase of growth for Balfour Beatty’s aviation portfolio.Trent Johnson holds a degree in Construction Engineering Management from North Carolina State University and is also known for his community involvement, particularly with the Make-A-Wish Foundation. His steady leadership style and collaborative approach have helped Balfour Beatty deliver projects that align with long-term regional growth and infrastructure priorities.

+ MAINMARK UK JOINS BALFOUR BEATTY'S CP7 FRAMEWORK FOR RAIL INFRASTRUCTURE

Ground engineering specialist Mainmark UK has secured a place on the Balfour Beatty plc CP7 Framework, positioning the company to support the delivery of key rail infrastructure works across Network Rail’s latest five-year investment programme. This investment period, known as Control Period 7 (CP7), spans from 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2029, with an allocation of approximately £43–45 billion across Great Britain.The CP7 programme focuses on maintaining, renewing, and modernising the rail network to deliver a safer, more reliable, and more sustainable railway. Investment priorities during CP7 include infrastructure maintenance and renewals, digital signalling and technology, safety improvements, and initiatives aimed at enhancing both freight and passenger performance across the network.As part of the CP7 Framework, contractors will engage in a variety of works aligned with Network Rail’s Strategic Business Plan. These activities will encompass low-complexity emergency callouts, planned preventative maintenance, and small-scale civil engineering schemes. Mainmark UK will be involved in geotechnical response works, bridge repairs, structural integrity projects, platform refurbishments, and drainage clearance, among others.Tom Kavanagh, General Manager at Mainmark UK, expressed enthusiasm about the collaboration with Balfour Beatty, stating, “We’re looking forward to collaborating with Balfour Beatty to help deliver critical infrastructure works across the network during the CP7 period. Being selected to support Balfour Beatty on this framework is a fantastic opportunity for our team and aligns perfectly with our growth plans.”Mainmark is known for its innovative ground engineering solutions, particularly its Teretek® resin injection technology, which stabilises weak ground conditions and fills subsurface voids without extensive excavation. This technology is crucial for maintaining the integrity of rail infrastructure, especially as heavier freight loads and severe weather events put increasing pressure on railway assets. With its appointment to the CP7 Framework, Mainmark UK is set to play a vital role in ensuring the resilience and reliability of the UK's rail network.

+ BALFOUR BEATTY'S GROWTH FUELED BY RECORD ORDER BOOK

Balfour Beatty PLC has reported impressive growth, driven by its robust performance in the construction sector, particularly in power transmission projects. The company’s market valuation has soared nearly two-thirds over the past year, a noteworthy achievement for a FTSE 250 constituent. This growth trajectory is largely attributed to a record order book and a 9 percent increase in underlying earnings per share, which reached 47.6 pence.On 11th March 2026, the construction group revealed that its top-line growth was significantly supported by the increasing demand for power transmission in the UK and a surge in building projects in the US. Operating profits within Balfour Beatty's support services segment saw a remarkable increase of one-third, amounting to £122 million, alongside an 80 basis point rise in the underlying margin to 8.5 percent. The company’s UK construction division achieved its margin targets ahead of schedule, underscoring its operational efficiency.In the US market, Balfour Beatty has capitalized on rising building volumes although it faced challenges due to cost overruns in a project within its Civils arm. The dual market strategy appears to be paying off, as the company navigates through these hurdles while maintaining a focus on profitability. The expansion of infrastructure projects, particularly in power transmission, has provided a solid foundation for growth, enabling Balfour Beatty to solidify its position in the competitive construction landscape.Market analysts remain optimistic about Balfour Beatty’s future prospects, considering its substantial order book which continues to grow amidst a backdrop of increasing infrastructure investment. The company’s strategic initiatives to enhance operational margins and deliver projects on time have positioned it well for sustained growth in the coming quarters. Investors are likely to monitor Balfour Beatty’s performance closely as it seeks to leverage its strengths in both the UK and US markets.As Balfour Beatty continues to capitalize on the opportunities presented by the expanding power grid and overall construction demand, it remains crucial for stakeholders to assess the potential impacts of rising costs and project management challenges. Overall, the company’s recent performance indicates a strong recovery trajectory, making it a noteworthy player in the construction sector as it heads into the next fiscal year.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO [3 News That Matters]

+ TRACK3D SECURES MAJOR PARTNERSHIP WITH HENSEL PHELPS FOR CONSTRUCTION TECHNOLOGY

Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, has marked a significant milestone by securing a multi-year agreement with Hensel Phelps, expanding its collaboration from a single pilot to the deployment of Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform across more than 200 active projects. This announcement was made on 1st April 2026, emphasizing Track3D's growing influence in the construction sector.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize the use of Track3D for portfolio-wide progress monitoring, indicating strong confidence in the platform’s scalability and effectiveness across diverse construction projects. By utilizing automated progress tracking, Hensel Phelps aims to identify on-site issues earlier, which can significantly reduce delays and enhance schedule reliability, especially in complex builds.A notable case study from San Francisco International Airport highlighted the successful application of Track3D’s technology on a challenging infrastructure project. Project leaders reported a shift from reactive issue resolution to proactive decision-making, facilitated by 360deg site walks and the involvement of existing field personnel, thereby minimizing the need for major workflow changes. This deployment at SFO reinforces Track3D's relevance in high-value infrastructure work and solidifies its position in the reality intelligence market.In addition to the partnership with Hensel Phelps, Track3D has introduced a March update to its platform that focuses on large-enterprise oversight and workflow efficiency. Key enhancements include an admin dashboard and Groups functionality, which are designed to provide major contractors with centralized portfolio visibility and streamlined user management. Furthermore, the new capture-free progress tracking feature allows teams to document job site status directly on floorplans, promoting continuous oversight.Track3D is also intensifying its go-to-market strategy by targeting specialty contractors, with plans to participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026. The company aims to showcase tools that automate production tracking and reduce manual reporting for drywall and ceiling professionals. Collectively, these developments—ranging from the Hensel Phelps portfolio rollout to enterprise-focused product enhancements—underscore Track3D’s strengthening competitive position in construction technology and may pave the way for sustainable growth in the future.

+ MICAH CHOY STEPS UP AS PROJECT SUPERINTENDENT AT HENSEL PHELPS

Micah (Pono) Choy has been promoted to project superintendent at Hensel Phelps, overseeing the Hawaii Department of Education’s Regional Kitchen – North Central Oahu project in Wahiawa. As of 1st April 2026, Choy's promotion marks a significant milestone in his career with the company, which he joined in 2018.During his tenure at Hensel Phelps, Choy has played a pivotal role in various major projects across the aviation, wastewater, and federal market sectors in Hawaii and Guam. His collaborative leadership style has garnered recognition, allowing him to build strong partnerships across teams and effectively drive project outcomes.Choy's promotion to project superintendent reflects Hensel Phelps' commitment to developing talent within the organization. His experience and expertise will be instrumental in managing the Regional Kitchen project, which is critical for enhancing educational facilities in Hawaii.The construction industry continues to evolve, and Hensel Phelps is positioning itself for future growth by promoting leaders like Choy. His ability to navigate complex projects while fostering teamwork aligns with the company's strategic goals.As Hensel Phelps embarks on new initiatives, Choy's leadership will likely contribute to the successful execution of high-profile projects. Stakeholders and investors will be watching closely as the company continues to build its reputation in the construction sector.

+ TRACK3D PARTNERS WITH HENSEL PHELPS FOR MAJOR CONSTRUCTION ROLLOUT

Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, has announced a significant milestone in its growth trajectory as it secures a multi-year enterprise agreement with Hensel Phelps. As of 25th March 2026, this collaboration expands from a single pilot project to deployment across more than 200 active projects, marking a notable shift in how the construction industry embraces advanced technology.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform portfolio-wide for progress monitoring, demonstrating a strong confidence in the technology's scalability across diverse project types. This extensive rollout is expected to deepen recurring revenue streams for Track3D, enhance customer loyalty, and establish a high-profile reference account that could attract additional large contractor deals.In addition to the enterprise agreement, Track3D highlighted a successful case study at San Francisco International Airport. Hensel Phelps utilized Track3D's technology on this demanding build, where project leaders noted that the platform facilitated a shift from reactive to proactive decision-making. + PCL CONSTRUCTION TACKLES COLORADO RIVER CRISIS WITH INNOVATIVE WATER SOLUTIONS

As the Colorado River faces unprecedented challenges, PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. is stepping up to provide innovative solutions aimed at water resilience. On the 26th of March 2026, the urgency surrounding the Colorado River's dwindling water supply became a focal point for communities across the Western United States, as they seek alternatives to secure their water futures.The Colorado River serves as a vital resource for millions, yet it is currently grappling with severe drought conditions. Lake Powell, for instance, is at an alarming 162 feet below full capacity, holding only 26% of its total water volume. The U.S. Bureau of Reclamation warns that if current conditions persist, Lake Mead could reach record lows within two years, prompting a critical reassessment of water management strategies across the basin states.PCL Construction is at the forefront of this reassessment, advocating for investments in water reuse infrastructure as a key strategy for bolstering water security. With projected U.S. investments in water reuse expected to average $47.1 billion over the next decade, PCL is positioned to support municipalities in capturing and reusing water to mitigate the effects of ongoing drought. Although adoption rates vary significantly among the seven Colorado River basin states, PCL's initiatives exemplify how infrastructure investments can enhance resilience in water supply systems.The urgency of the situation is underscored by the imminent expiration of current water management rules in the fall of 2026. As states grapple with the looming deadline to ratify Colorado River water allocation agreements, PCL Construction's projects, such as the Drought Pipeline Program in Phoenix, provide tangible examples of how communities can adapt to a drier future. This program has been crucial in ensuring the reliability of water supply for the rapidly growing Phoenix area, which has seen a 12% population increase over the last decade.Industry leaders, including PCL, recently gathered at the Colorado River Water Users Association Conference to discuss collaborative strategies for securing the river's future. The consensus was clear: a multifaceted approach is necessary to address the complexities of climate change and aging infrastructure. PCL Construction's commitment to advancing water reuse initiatives positions the company as a critical player in shaping sustainable water management practices that can withstand the challenges posed by the Colorado River's ongoing crisis.

The resolution highlights the safety concerns of students who currently navigate busy streets to access essential facilities, advocating for collaboration with the Kentucky Department of Transportation for this project.Further addressing financial constraints faced by students, Bill 17-26-S was also approved, establishing the Textbooks for Toppers reimbursement program. This initiative will provide $50 reimbursements for students purchasing required course materials, a move that reflects the SGA's recognition of the financial challenges many students encounter. Additionally, other bills passed will provide essential academic supplies, such as Scantrons and lab goggles, ensuring that students have access to necessary materials without added financial burden.As WKU moves forward with its partnership with Gilbane and implements these new funding initiatives, the SGA's commitment to improving the student experience is clear. The collaboration with Gilbane not only aims to enhance student housing but also signifies a broader strategy to address various student needs and concerns, fostering a supportive academic environment.

+ GILBANE LAUNCHES LEASING FOR $135M AFFORDABLE HOUSING PROJECT IN D.C.

WASHINGTON, D.C. — Gilbane Development has begun leasing the second of its three-phase redevelopment at 7th Street and Barnaby Road in Southeast Washington, D.C. As of 3rd April 2026, Phase II of Barnaby & 7th includes 229 units within four new residential buildings: The Stoneview, The Solomon, The Marte, and The Lewis.Construction of these buildings is slated for completion by summer 2026. The four communities feature a mix of studio to three-bedroom units serving households earning 50 and 80 percent of the area median income (AMI). Notably, twenty percent of the apartments will be set aside as Permanent Supportive Housing (PSH) units, which will be managed by the district’s Department of Homeless Services.Phase I of Barnaby & 7th consisted of The Upland, which was completed in spring 2025 and includes 169 apartments, with 34 designated as PSH units. The income restrictions for this phase ranged from 30 to 50 percent of AMI. The third phase of development is expected to include approximately 100 additional rental units in one multifamily building, bringing the project to a grand total of 498 units upon completion.During the construction of Phase II, Gilbane also built a new pedestrian walkway designed to connect all six buildings of the larger development. According to the District of Columbia Housing Finance Agency, the total project cost for Phase II was approximately $135 million, underscoring Gilbane's commitment to affordable housing in the region.As the project progresses, it is anticipated to significantly contribute to the availability of affordable housing options in Washington, D.C., while also enhancing community connectivity and providing essential support services for residents in need.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLY REINER CACERES JOINS CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP AS HR DIRECTOR

Carly Reiner Caceres has been appointed as the Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction Group, bringing a wealth of experience in people operations and talent strategy. On the 29th of April 2026, Caceres officially began her new role, where she will be instrumental in supporting national campus recruiting initiatives aimed at bolstering early-career talent pipelines for one of the largest building and infrastructure companies in the United States.Prior to her appointment at Clark Construction Group, Caceres served as the Vice President of People Operations at Proper, where she was pivotal in shaping the HR strategy and operational support within a hybrid work environment. Her extensive background includes significant roles at MILLER as the Director of Internal Operations and more than nine years at Accenture, where she excelled in various capacities including Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Caceres’ career trajectory showcases her expertise in full-cycle recruiting, stakeholder management, and employer branding, which will be crucial as Clark Construction Group seeks to attract top talent from universities across the nation. Her previous experience managing campus recruiting strategies at Grant Thornton LLP further solidifies her capabilities in building robust internship programs and enhancing student engagement initiatives.Clark Construction Group, founded in 1906, has established a strong reputation for delivering value across major markets, including education and infrastructure. With a commitment to community strengthening and project excellence, the company is poised to benefit from Caceres' strategic insights and leadership in HR, particularly in nurturing the next generation of professionals in the construction industry.Caceres’ early career involved internships and roles with various organizations, including Charleston Magazine and the Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum, which helped her build a diverse skill set in communications and external relations. As she embarks on this new chapter at Clark Construction Group, her multifaceted background is expected to contribute significantly to the company's growth and talent development objectives.

+ CLARK CONTRACTS SECURES MULTI-TRADES FRAMEWORK WITH RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL

Clark Contracts has been appointed to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades contractors framework, enhancing the delivery of repair, maintenance, and refurbishment services to domestic properties across the local authority area. This announcement was made on 11th February 2026.The framework, which is set to run until 2027, encompasses a variety of multi-trade works. This will enable Renfrewshire Council to effectively manage both responsive and planned maintenance services across its housing stock, ensuring that local residents receive prompt and reliable service.Clark Contracts is already an established player in the public sector, holding several framework agreements that include Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework. Additionally, the company is involved with the Crown Commercial Service’s Construction Framework and various Scottish Procurement Alliance frameworks focusing on Public Buildings, New Build Housing, and more.Gordon Cunningham, managing director at Clark Contracts, expressed enthusiasm about the new appointment, saying, "Building on our strong track record of delivering more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over the past three years, we are pleased to have been appointed to Renfrewshire Council’s framework. As a Paisley-based business, we are proud to be supporting the council and delivering refurbishment and maintenance services that directly benefit local communities."The addition of this framework is expected to bolster Clark Contracts' already significant presence in the public sector, as the company continues to collaborate with various councils and institutions, including the University of Glasgow and Glasgow City Council, to deliver essential services.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP ADVANCES ₱4-B MIXED-USE HUB AT THE VILLAGES

CLARK FREEPORT – A ₱4-billion mixed-use residential and commercial development in The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination here, has been given the green light with the approval by the Clark Development Corp. of an amendment to PKR Corp.’s lease agreement on 13th December 2025. This ambitious project underscores Clark Construction Group's aggressive strategy to attract significant investments and fortify its reputation as Asia’s Leading Meetings and Conference Destination, a title recently bestowed by the World Travel Awards.The revised lease agreement provides PKR Corp. with a 30-month timeline to complete construction and incorporates stringent performance safeguards to ensure timely project delivery. Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera, president and CEO of Clark Development Corp. (CDC), emphasized that the amendment reflects the organization's commitment to fostering investor confidence and efficient execution of projects. "With this agreement, we ensure that it is a good place for everyone to go and with our coordination, we want to be able to maintain that area [The Villages], an area of preference, where people can reside and really be part of the community," Devanadera stated.PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung expressed gratitude for the partnership with CDC, stating, "We are honored to take part in Clark’s growth and with CDC as our partner, we will deliver this development in Clark." PKR Corp. operates under the umbrella of Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean firm known for its expertise in building, civil, residential, and infrastructure projects. The company has received accolades for safety, technology, and workplace practices from various esteemed organizations including the Korea Expressway Corp. and the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy.This mixed-use development is part of CDC’s broader strategy to expedite investments, promote well-planned communities, and establish a business-friendly environment that appeals to both global and local investors. The focus on creating integrated lifestyle hubs aligns with the increasing demand for residential options that complement commercial and recreational spaces, positioning Clark as an attractive destination for future developments.Clark Construction Group's proactive approach in securing this project represents a significant step forward in the region's economic landscape, potentially catalyzing further investments and developments. As the construction timeline unfolds, stakeholders will be keenly observing how this initiative contributes to the overall growth and vibrancy of The Villages and the Clark Freeport Zone.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JHV-2T23-S4NP-P07H-00000-00Interim Report Analysis Half Year ending 31 March 2026: Cabot EPS drops 22.1% Corporate Wire Date: 06 May 2026 09:42 EDT Release Date: May 05, 20262026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:57ZLexisNexisInterim Report Analysis Half Year ending 31 March 2026: Cabot EPS drops 22.1% Corporate Wire Date: 06 May 2026 09:42 EDT Release Date: May 05, 2026

AMERICAN RESULTS

Cabot (NYSE:CBT) announced EPS of $2.65 for the half year-ended 31 March 2026, down 22.1% from $3.4 in the previous corresponding period.

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Unfavourable Changes:

- Net profit slumped 24.6% from $187.0m to $141.0m

- Total revenue was down 7.3% from $1.9b to $1.8b

- EPS slumped 22.1% from $3.4 to $2.65

- EBIT to Revenues was down from 15.6% to 13.6%

- Profit before tax to Revenues was down from 15.6% to 13.6%

- Profit after tax to Revenues was down from 9.9% to 8%

Half-Yearly Report (31 Mar 2026)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; 31 Mar 2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] Revenue was down 7.3% and Net Profit was down 24.6%.

Half year-ended31 Mar 202631 Mar 2025EPS, $2.63.4PCP growth in EPS %-22.142.3Revenue, $ billion1.81.9PCP growth in Revenue %-7.3-4.4Net Profit, $ million141187PCP growth in Net Profit %-24.639.6

ACTIVITIES:

Cabot Corporation is a global leader in specialty chemicals and performance materials, headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts, and listed on the New York Stock Exchange. The company's core business activities focus on the production and supply of advanced materials such as carbon black, which is critical for tires, industrial rubber, and plastics; fumed silica for adhesives, coatings, and electronics; and specialty additives for energy storage and inkjet applications. These offerings serve diverse industries including automotive, construction, and electronics, emphasizing innovation in sustainable and high-performance solutions. The fastest-growing segments include battery materials and sustainable products, fueled by rising demand for electric vehicles, renewable energy technologies, and eco-friendly alternatives. With a commitment to research and development, Cabot maintains a strong global footprint, operating in over 20 countries and generating revenue through strategic partnerships and operational efficiency.

MARKET POSITION:

Cabot Corporation (NYSE:CBT), the NYSE's 19th largest Chemicals company by market cap, increased $3.31 (or 4.4%) in the trailing 12 months to May 05, 2026 to close at $78.93. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 27.6% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -23.3%. Average daily volume of 428,362 shares in the trailing year was 1.1 times average daily volume in the previous year.

urn:contentItem:6JHK-Y103-S8YC-S2Y6-00000-00Interim Report Analysis Half Year ending 31 March 2026: Cabot EPS drops 22.1% Corporate Wire Date: 06 May 2026 09:42 EDT Release Date: May 05, 20262026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:57ZLexisNexisInterim Report Analysis Half Year ending 31 March 2026: Cabot EPS drops 22.1% Corporate Wire Date: 06 May 2026 09:42 EDT Release Date: May 05, 2026

AMERICAN RESULTS

Cabot (NYSE:CBT) announced EPS of $2.65 for the half year-ended 31 March 2026, down 22.1% from $3.4 in the previous corresponding period.

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Unfavourable Changes:

- Net profit slumped 24.6% from $187.0m to $141.0m

- Total revenue was down 7.3% from $1.9b to $1.8b

- EPS slumped 22.1% from $3.4 to $2.65

- EBIT to Revenues was down from 15.6% to 13.6%

- Profit before tax to Revenues was down from 15.6% to 13.6%

- Profit after tax to Revenues was down from 9.9% to 8%

Half-Yearly Report (31 Mar 2026)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; 31 Mar 2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] Revenue was down 7.3% and Net Profit was down 24.6%.

Half year-ended31 Mar 202631 Mar 2025EPS, $2.63.4PCP growth in EPS %-22.142.3Revenue, $ billion1.81.9PCP growth in Revenue %-7.3-4.4Net Profit, $ million141187PCP growth in Net Profit %-24.639.6

ACTIVITIES:

Cabot Corporation is a global leader in specialty chemicals and performance materials, headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts, and listed on the New York Stock Exchange. The company's core business activities focus on the production and supply of advanced materials such as carbon black, which is critical for tires, industrial rubber, and plastics; fumed silica for adhesives, coatings, and electronics; and specialty additives for energy storage and inkjet applications. These offerings serve diverse industries including automotive, construction, and electronics, emphasizing innovation in sustainable and high-performance solutions. The fastest-growing segments include battery materials and sustainable products, fueled by rising demand for electric vehicles, renewable energy technologies, and eco-friendly alternatives. With a commitment to research and development, Cabot maintains a strong global footprint, operating in over 20 countries and generating revenue through strategic partnerships and operational efficiency.

MARKET POSITION:

Cabot Corporation (NYSE:CBT), the NYSE's 19th largest Chemicals company by market cap, increased $3.31 (or 4.4%) in the trailing 12 months to May 05, 2026 to close at $78.93. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 27.6% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -23.3%. Average daily volume of 428,362 shares in the trailing year was 1.1 times average daily volume in the previous year.

Cabot (NYSE:CBT) announced EPS of $2.65 for the half year-ended 31 March 2026, down 22.1% from $3.4 in the previous corresponding period.

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Unfavourable Changes:

- Net profit slumped 24.6% from $187.0m to $141.0m

- Total revenue was down 7.3% from $1.9b to $1.8b

- EPS slumped 22.1% from $3.4 to $2.65

- EBIT to Revenues was down from 15.6% to 13.6%

- Profit before tax to Revenues was down from 15.6% to 13.6%

- Profit after tax to Revenues was down from 9.9% to 8%

Half-Yearly Report (31 Mar 2026)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; 31 Mar 2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] Revenue was down 7.3% and Net Profit was down 24.6%.

Half year-ended31 Mar 202631 Mar 2025EPS, $2.63.4PCP growth in EPS %-22.142.3Revenue, $ billion1.81.9PCP growth in Revenue %-7.3-4.4Net Profit, $ million141187PCP growth in Net Profit %-24.639.6

ACTIVITIES:

Cabot Corporation is a global leader in specialty chemicals and performance materials, headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts, and listed on the New York Stock Exchange. The company's core business activities focus on the production and supply of advanced materials such as carbon black, which is critical for tires, industrial rubber, and plastics; fumed silica for adhesives, coatings, and electronics; and specialty additives for energy storage and inkjet applications. These offerings serve diverse industries including automotive, construction, and electronics, emphasizing innovation in sustainable and high-performance solutions. The fastest-growing segments include battery materials and sustainable products, fueled by rising demand for electric vehicles, renewable energy technologies, and eco-friendly alternatives. With a commitment to research and development, Cabot maintains a strong global footprint, operating in over 20 countries and generating revenue through strategic partnerships and operational efficiency.

MARKET POSITION:

Cabot Corporation (NYSE:CBT), the NYSE's 19th largest Chemicals company by market cap, increased $3.31 (or 4.4%) in the trailing 12 months to May 05, 2026 to close at $78.93. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 27.6% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -23.3%. Average daily volume of 428,362 shares in the trailing year was 1.1 times average daily volume in the previous year.

value.Title: Ameresco Spins Off Biofuels Business Into HASI JV

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJD-BM73-S7PH-455W-00000-00Ameresco Spins Off Biofuels Business Into HASI JV2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:47ZLexisNexisAmeresco Spins Off Biofuels Business Into HASI JVby Mihaela Miteva

Ameresco Inc. (AMRC) and HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital Inc. (HASI) on Monday, May 4, said they've agreed to spin off Ameresco's biofuels business into a new joint venture, Neogenyx Fuels.The deal values Neogenyx Fuels at a post-money enterprise value of $1.8 billion.

Ameresco will own 70% of the venture, while HASI will own the remaining 30%.HASI has committed $400 million to the transaction. Of that amount, $300 million will be invested directly in Neogenyx Fuels to support growth, while $100 million will be paid to Ameresco for the existing business. Framingham, Mass.-based Ameresco said it will use the proceeds for strategic opportunities, working capital and deleveraging.Neogenyx Fuels will develop, own and operate advanced fuel assets aimed at supporting the energy transition. Michael T. Bakas, president of renewable fuels at Ameresco, will be CEO of the new company.HASI president and CEO Jeffrey A. Lipson said in the statement the transaction builds on the company's relationship with Ameresco, including more than 60 joint transactions since 2001.Ameresco provides energy infrastructure and efficiency services, upgrades aging infrastructure and develops distributed energy resources for federal, state and local governments, utilities, data centers, schools, healthcare institutions, housing authorities and commercial and industrial customers. In 2023, it picked up Italian renewable energy and energy efficiency company Enerqos Energy Solutions Srl.Annapolis, Md.-based HASI, previously called Hannon Armstrong Sustainable Infrastructure Capital Inc., invests in sustainable infrastructure assets, including utility-scale solar, storage, onshore wind, distributed solar and storage, renewable natural gas and energy efficiency. In May 2024, HASI and KKR & Co. (KKR) formed CarbonCount Holdings 1 LLC, with a joint commitment to invest up to $2 billion in climate-positive projects across the U.S.The companies expect to close the Neogenyx Fuels transaction this quarter.Ameresco turned to Guggenheim Securities LLC for financial advice and to Cyril V. Jones, J. Heath Mayo and David B. Feirstein of Kirkland & Ellis LLP for counsel.Gregory Hort and Sergio de la Torre of Lazard Ltd. provided financial advice to HASI, which looked to Daniel S. Alterbaum, Nicholas H. Politan Jr. and Jonathan R. Sapp of Gibson, Dunn &Crutcher LLP for counsel.

$ 1-10 Billion

urn:contentItem:6JHC-YV13-SHFW-V510-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Cabot reports 6.8% sequential fall in Quarterly Net Profit Corporate Wire Date: 06 May 2026 09:42 EDT Release Date: May 05, 20262026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:58ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Cabot reports 6.8% sequential fall in Quarterly Net Profit Corporate Wire Date: 06 May 2026 09:42 EDT Release Date: May 05, 2026

AMERICAN RESULTS

Cabot (NYSE:CBT), announced net profit of $68.0m for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q2/2026], down 6.8% from the previous quarter [Q1/2026] and down 28% from the year-earlier period [Q2/2025]. Earnings per share (EPS) were down 8% sequentially from $1.38 in Q1/2026 to $1.27 in Q2/2026.

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Unfavourable Changes:

- Net profit slumped 27.7% from $94.0m to $68.0m

- Total revenue was down 3.4% from $936.0m to $904.0m

- EPS slumped 25.7% from $1.71 to $1.27

- EBIT to Revenues was down from 16.1% to 13.3%

- Profit before tax to Revenues was down from 16.1% to 13.3%

- Profit after tax to Revenues was down from 10% to 7.5%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Quarter-ended31 Mar 202631 Dec 202530 Sep 2025EPS, c12713880Sequential growth in EPS %-8.072-57Revenue, $ million904849899Sequential growth in Revenue %6.5-5.6-2.6Net Profit, $ million68.073.043.0Sequential growth in Net Profit %-6.870-57

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q2/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was down 25.7%, Revenue was down 3.4% and Net Profit was down 27.7%.

Quarter-ended31 Mar 202631 Mar 2025EPS, c127171PCP growth in EPS %-2614Revenue, $ million904936PCP growth in Revenue %-3.4-8.1Net Profit, $ million68.094.0PCP growth in Net Profit %-27.711.9

ACTIVITIES:

Cabot Corporation is a global leader in specialty chemicals and performance materials, headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts, and listed on the New York Stock Exchange. The company's core business activities focus on the production and supply of advanced materials such as carbon black, which is critical for tires, industrial rubber, and plastics; fumed silica for adhesives, coatings, and electronics; and specialty additives for energy storage and inkjet applications. These offerings serve diverse industries including automotive, construction, and electronics, emphasizing innovation in sustainable and high-performance solutions. The fastest-growing segments include battery materials and sustainable products, fueled by rising demand for electric vehicles, renewable energy technologies, and eco-friendly alternatives. With a commitment to research and development, Cabot maintains a strong global footprint, operating in over 20 countries and generating revenue through strategic partnerships and operational efficiency.

MARKET POSITION:

Cabot Corporation (NYSE:CBT), the NYSE's 19th largest Chemicals company by market cap, increased $3.31 (or 4.4%) in the trailing 12 months to May 05, 2026 to close at $78.93. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 27.6% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -23.3%. Average daily volume of 428,362 shares in the trailing year was 1.1 times average daily volume in the previous year.

value.Title: How Mass. can balance data center growth and climate goals

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGH-3JX3-RVXM-V1M6-00000-00How Mass. can balance data center growth and climate goals2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:57ZLexisNexisOP-EDSHow Mass. can balance data center growth and climate goalsMindy LubberABSTRACT

The data center boom doesn't have to be a zero-sum competition between community groups and Big Tech. It can work for everyone if done right.

FULL TEXT

Link to Image

Mindy Lubber is CEO and president of Ceres,a Boston-based sustainability nonprofit advocacy organization.

The data center backlash has arrived. The Lowell City Council enacted a one-year local ban after a dispute about expanding the state's largest data center, and officials in Everett are poised to follow suit.

Communities across the United States stalled or blocked 48 data center projects worth at least $156 billion in 2025, often after locals took issue with costs, water use, noise, or pollution from diesel generators or natural gas power generation.

These concerns are legitimate, but the AI-driven data center boom doesn't have to be a zero-sum competition between community groups and Big Tech. It can work for everyone if done right.

States with strong clean energy and climate policies like Massachusetts must make sure the industry has a path to develop sustainably - with renewable energy at the forefront in the planning process. With the right approach, data centers can enable more wind, solar, and battery deployment and help the Commonwealth modernize its aging power grid.

A legislative package based on three principles - clean energy, community buy-in, and water efficiency - would deliver on Governor Maura Healey's efforts to expand the Massachusetts economy by embracing the AI revolution while balancing affordability and pollution-reduction goals.

First, Massachusetts should explicitly tie data center growth to clean energy and grid reliability. Data centers already account for 4 percent of energy usage nationally, and the newest AI facilities use as much power as entire cities. That kind of demand spike can drive up electricity rates and lead to more planet-warming pollution when it's tied to energy generated using fossil fuels.

Massachusetts lawmakers should require large power users to help pay for grid upgrades and to reduce demand during peak periods.

State Representative Rodney Elliott, who represents Lowell, has proposed a solid starting point. His bill would prevent rate hikes associated with data centers and require transparency about the cost of powering these facilities and hooking them up to the grid.

The Commonwealth should also establish a statewide community benefits framework that ensures nearby residents share the economic gains from data centers and have meaningful influence over project design. Potential benefits include increased tax revenue and job opportunities, as well as financial support for local grid upgrades and energy efficiency programs, funded by data center operators. Communities like Lowell and Everett should not be left to fight billion-dollar companies on their own or reinvent the rules city-by-city.

Instead, the process needs to be clear from the outset. Developers should be required to consult with communities early on and to make investments in local infrastructure and noise mitigation at their facilities. Massachusetts also needs dedicated funding for municipalities to conduct data center oversight. At the end of the day, what communities want is transparency and responsible planning.

And finally, if Massachusetts wants to get ahead of future risks in a warming world, water planning needs to be central to data center policy. More than 90 percent of the Commonwealth is currently in a drought. Data centers require large amounts of water for cooling and to generate the power they need. Policy makers should require both existing and proposed data centers to publicly disclose key water information, including onsite water use and water impacts tied to electricity generation. And they should enact clear statewide standards for water efficiency and maximizing use of non-potable water. Communities should have a voice in identifying and addressing potential impacts.

Healey recently said she wants Massachusetts to "embrace" data centers and to build them "in a way that doesn't harm our residents."

She's right. Massachusetts should make itself a hub for the innovative new digital economy, and it should set the standard for building data centers the right way.

In this file photo made available by Google, hundreds of fans funnel hot air from the computer servers into a cooling unit to be recirculated at a Google data center in Mayes County, Okla.

urn:contentItem:6JJP-KX53-RY52-61C7-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - INDUSTRY & PEER ROUNDUP: KEY MARKET MOVEMENTS2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:53:58ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - INDUSTRY & PEER ROUNDUP: KEY MARKET MOVEMENTS

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ PILLSBURY PREVAILS IN WINERY DISPUTE AFTER FIVE-DAY JURY TRIAL

Ateam of Pillsbury lawyers led by Austin-based Litigation partner Casey Low prevailed in a five-day jury trial in favor of client Fred Schrader, the founder of the highly acclaimed Schrader Cellars winery, and the winery's current owner.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURY OPENS IN BOSTON WITH A FOCUS ON TECHNOLOGY, LIFE SCIENCES AND FINANCIAL INDUSTRIES

Pillsbury is opening a new office in Boston, marking an important expansion into one of the nation's leading growth and innovation centers. The office will launch with a team of five senior attorneys and additional associates, who bring diverse transactional and litigation backgrounds.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURYS MARQUEE INSURANCE RECOVERY & ADVISORY PRACTICE CONTINUES EXPANSION, WELCOMING RENOWNED POLICYHOLDER LITIGATOR BOB GILBERT

Robert J. Gilbert, a nationally recognized litigator who represents policyholders in complex insurance recovery matters, has joined Pillsbury from Latham & Watkins. He comes to the firm as one of the founding partners of Pillsbury's newly established Boston office.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 Press Releases]

+ KING & SPALDING STEPHEN VAUGHN NAMED ONE OF D.C.S 500 MOST INFLUENTIAL PEOPLE BY WASHINGTONIAN MAGAZINE

King &Spalding Partner Stephen Vaughn has been named one of Washington, D.C.'s 500 Most Influential People by Washingtonian magazine.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE REPRESENTS SEDRON IN INVESTMENT FROM ARA PARTNERS OF UP TO $500 MILLION

PORTLAND, OR (April 15, 2026)-Perkins Coie announced today that it represented Sedron Technologies, a leader in advanced upcycling systems that transform waste streams into reusable water and fertilizer products, in an investment of up to $500 million from Ara Partners, a global private equity and infrastructure firm focused on industrial decarbonization.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE STRENGTHENS PACIFIC NORTHWEST TRIAL BENCH WITH COMMERCIAL LITIGATOR CHAD COLTON

PORTLAND, OR and SEATTLE, WA (April 6, 2026)-Perkins Coie is pleased to announce that Chad Colton has joined its Commercial Litigation practice as a partner in Portland and Seattle, further strengthening the firm's trial capabilities in the Pacific Northwest.

Bringing two decades of experience, Chad represents clients in high-stakes commercial matters such as partnership, shareholder, breach of contract, IP, and licensing disputes. He also frequently serves as lead trial counsel for bet-the-company cases, often joining to advise and advocate for clients in the middle of litigation or immediately before trial.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ COOLEY LLP VETHOS ANNOUNCES $200 MILLION IPO

Cooley advised Ethos Technologies, a top life insurance technology company on a mission to democratize access to life insurance, on its $200 million initial public offering (IPO). Ethos offered 5,127,696 shares of Class A common stock, and selling stockholders offered an additional 5,398,619 shares of Class A common stock, at a price of $19 per share. Ethos began trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on January 29, 2026, under the ticker symbol LIFE.

Partner Matthew Bartus led the Cooley team advising Ethos since its formation in 2016, including on its $100 million Series D-1 in July 2021 and $200 million Series D in May 2021.

Lawyers Jon Avina, Jean Park, Milson Yu, Samara Zaifman, Katie Lapidus and Charlotte Yin led the Cooley team advising Ethos on its IPO.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP NEUROPHOS SECURES $110 MILLION SERIES A

Cooley advised Neurophos, a leader in photonic artificial intelligence chip technology, on its oversubscribed $110 million Series A round, bringing total funding to $118 million. The new funding will accelerate the delivery of Neurophos' first integrated photonic compute system, including datacenter-ready optical processing unit modules, a full software stack and early access developer hardware.

The round was led by Gates Frontier, with participation from M12 (Microsoft's venture fund), Carbon Direct Capital, Aramco Ventures, Bosch Ventures, Tectonic Ventures, Space Capital and others.

Lawyers James Allen, Nick Hobson, James Reizman and Nathan Alamillo led the Cooley team advising Neurophos.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP OPENEVIDENCE RAISES $250 MILLION SERIES D

Cooley advised OpenEvidence, the most widely used artificial intelligence (AI) platform by doctors in America, on its $250 million Series D financing, valuing the company at $12 billion and making it the most valuable healthcare AI company in the world. The new funding will be used to invest heavily in the research and development and compute costs associated with the multi-AI agentic architecture of OpenEvidence, which provides the highest quality and most accurate medical answers of any system in the world.

The round was co-led by Thrive Capital and DST Global, and brings the total funding raised by OpenEvidence to nearly $700 million over the past year.

Lawyers Derek Colla, David Safren and Sela Dougherty led the Cooley team advising OpenEvidence.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP INFERACT ANNOUNCES $150 MILLION SEED FINANCING

Cooley advised Inferact, a startup founded by creators and core maintainers of the open-source project vLLM, on its $150 million seed financing at an $800 million valuation. The financing was co-led by Andreessen Horowitz and Lightspeed Venture Partners, with investments from Sequoia Capital, Altimeter Capital, Redpoint Ventures, ZhenFund, The House Fund, Striker Venture Partners, Laude Ventures, Databricks Ventures and the UC Berkeley Chancellor's Fund.

Lawyers Peter Werner, Tara Capsuto, Won Lee, Carl Min and Isabella Ysebrands led the Cooley team advising Inferact.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ K&L GATES [3 Press Releases]

+ TWO K&L GATES PARTNERS AGAIN NAMED TO INDIA BUSINESS LAW JOURNALS INTERNATIONAL A-LIST 2026

K&L Gates is pleased to announce that Singapore Office Managing Partner Raja Bose and Singapore Corporate partner Meraj Noor have once again been named to India Business Law Journal's (IBLJ) International A-List. This marks the eighth consecutive year both partners have received this recognition.

IBLJ's International A-List identifies the top lawyers based outside of India who are recognized for their work on India-related matters. The A-List was compiled by IBLJ's seasoned editorial team following extensive market research, drawing on feedback from hundreds of lawyers at Indian law firms and thousands of India-focused in-house counsel within India and abroad.

The full International A-List 2026 is featured on IBLJ's website.

**All Singapore lawyers are members of K&L Gates Straits Law LLC, the Singapore office of global law firm K&L Gates. Source: Company Website

+ MORE THAN 80 K&L GATES LAWYERS ACROSS ASIA-PACIFIC RECOGNISED IN 2027 BEST LAWYERS, ONES TO WATCH EDITIONS

Best Lawyers®, a peer-review publication in the legal profession, has named more than 80 K&L Gates lawyers across the Asia-Pacific region in the 2027 editions of its guides, covering Australia, Japan, and Singapore. In addition, nearly 20 of the firm's lawyers were named as Best Lawyers: Ones to Watch® in Australia and Japan, lists that recognise lawyers early in their careers.

Alongside these recognitions, four Australian partners were recognised with "Lawyer of the Year" awards, an accolade that is particularly noteworthy, as it is only bestowed on one standout lawyer for each practice area in each jurisdiction.

The Best Lawyers® guides highlight top legal talent, selected by peers within the same geographical region and legal practice area, through millions of votes each year. Congratulations to the Lawyer of the Year award recipients below, as well as all lawyers recognised in the 2027 editions - resulting in nearly 180 total recognitions for the firm. Source: Company Website

+ K&L GATES ADVISES DOLPHIN ENTERTAINMENT, INC. ON US$15 MILLION PIPE INVESTMENT

Global law firm K&L Gates LLP advised Dolphin Entertainment, Inc., an entertainment marketing and premium content production company, in connection with a US$15 million private investment in public equity (PIPE) financing with Lincoln Park Capital, an investment group and asset management firm.

The Corporate team advising on the transaction was led by Miami partner Clayton Parker with support from Miami partner Erin Fogarty.

"This transaction underscores Dolphin Entertainment's commitment to accelerating strategic growth across its marketing and content-driven businesses. We were pleased to support them in securing investment from a partner aligned with its long-term vision," said Parker.

K&L Gates' Corporate practice is one of the most substantial in the legal industry, with hundreds of lawyers in offices across the globe providing clients with practical legal solutions in the structuring, financing, and completion of domestic, international, and cross-border transactions. Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ REED SMITH LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ REED SMITH ADVISES NATURPAK ON SALE BY TRIVEST TO PPC

CHICAGO - Global law firm Reed Smith represented Trivest Partners and IPMF, LLC, doing business as NaturPak, a manufacturer of bone broths, soups, sauces, wet pet food, and food ingredients for national retailers and brands, on its sale by Trivest Growth Investment Fund II to Pritzker Private Capital ("PPC"). Trivest Growth Investment Fund II, managed by Trivest Partners LP, a private equity firm focused exclusively on investments in founder- and family owned businesses, partnered with NaturPak through a minority investment in 2020. During Trivest's partnership, NaturPak transformed from a small specialty co-manufacturer into a scaled, multi-segment platform, increasing its R&D and manufacturing capabilities and production capacity.

The cross-office Reed Smith deal team included attorneys in Chicago, New York, Denver, Philadelphia, Dallas, Orange County (California), Pittsburgh, Washington, Houston, Tysons (Virginia), and Miami. The team was led by Nick Gibson, Kristen Capriotti, Crockett Stevenson, and Sudeep Kalkunte, with support from Constantine Karides, Brad Schmarak, David Hayes, Simon Kliegman, Ian Sherwin, Paulo McKeeby, Daniel Hagedorn, Michelle Mantine, Cori Goldberg, A.J. Wissinger, Jillian Burstein, Tyler Thompson, Mike Summers, Michael Jacobs, Brendan Leanos, Benjamin Brimeyer, Michael Galibois, Michael Lowell, Joseph Mammone Jr., Michelle DiPuma, Batool Banker, Leah Hungerman, Taber Rueter, Clirae Bourke, Michaela Mapes, Peter Loy, Talia Boiangin Behar, Elliott Race, George Avdellas, Katherine Litaker, Taylor Kollmansberger, and Nikita Bazaj.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH PARTNERS JOSH LOVE, ERIC MARDER, STEPHEN SESSA, AND EDWARD SHAPIRO NAMED TO VARIETY'S "DEALMAKERS 2025" REPORT

LOS ANGELES - Global law firm Reed Smith announced today that Josh Love, Eric Marder, Stephen Sessa, and Edward Shapiro, partners in the firm's global Entertainment & Media Industry Group, have been named to Variety's "Dealmakers 2025" report. Love, Marder, Sessa, and Shapiro are among an elite group of entertainment attorneys who have been chosen based on their more than $3 billion in music transactions in 2025, high‑profile client roster, and leadership across the music industry. The publication highlights their work on "sports and music transactions, start-ups and brands," "recording, music publishing, and live performance agreements (including Bad Bunny's upcoming Super Bowl halftime show)," and "catalog acquisitions."

This recognition reflects Reed Smith's continued leadership in high-stakes entertainment and music transactions, talent representation, and emerging legal issues at the intersection of entertainment, music, technology, and finance. The four partners were selected for their high-profile talent negotiations and representation business.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH ADVISES LEON CAPITAL ON STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN AVANTIS GROUP

LONDON - Global law firm Reed Smith has advised funds advised by Leon Capital on the strategic investment in Avantis Group, a UK-based provider of specialist engineering services to the maritime and energy industries. The transaction, which represents the latest new investment by Leon Capital, completed on 23 December 2025. The terms of the investment are confidential.

The transaction involved the Development Bank of Wales and Avantis Group's founders, Chris David and Tom David. Avantis Group's existing management team will retain operational control of the business. Blake Morgan acted as legal counsel to the Development Bank of Wales, while Acuity Law advised the founders. AMA Capital Partners acted as corporate finance adviser to Avantis Group.

Reed Smith advised Leon Capital on all aspects of the transaction, providing comprehensive M&A support in connection with the strategic investment. The matter involved a multi-disciplinary team spanning M&A, tax, and regulatory considerations.

The investment supports Avantis Group's long-term growth strategy across maritime and energy markets, including expansion into digital infrastructure and defence, and reflects Leon Capital's confidence in the company's differentiated engineering platform and experienced management team.

For Reed Smith, the transaction represents a further example of the firm's work advising private capital sponsors on complex M&A transactions and continues to strengthen its relationship with Leon Capital as it expands its platform.

The Reed Smith team advising on the matter was led by Andrew Houghton, with support from Ciaran Sweeney, Georgia Moorhouse and Selina Cook on M&A matters; Caspar Fox and Tom Baxter on tax; and Emma Weeden on FDI and antitrust.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH STRENGTHENS ASIA-PACIFIC FINANCE AND TRANSPORTATION CAPABILITY WITH HIRE IN SINGAPORE

SINGAPORE - Global law firm Reed Smith announced the appointment of banking and finance partner Dr. Gregory Xu to its Singapore office. Xu, who joins from Stephenson Harwood, where he led the general banking and project finance offering in Southeast Asia and served on the firm's Supervisory Council, its executive committee, will join Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group.

With over 15 years of experience, Xu advises on high-value, cross-border transactions across a wide range of sectors, including transportation - shipping, aviation, and logistics - trade, life sciences, energy, and technology. He regularly acts for financial institutions, funds, export credit agencies, leasing houses, asset owners, and Fortune 500 companies on complex financing, restructuring, and enforcement matters.

Xu has a strong track record in green and sustainability-linked lending and advises on multi-jurisdictional insolvency-related enforcement and restructuring matters. He also brings extensive experience in general corporate and commercial matters.

Richard Hakes, chair of Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group, said: "Gregory is an outstanding addition to our team, bringing deep knowledge of ship finance, aviation finance, and banking throughout Southeast Asia. His arrival further strengthens our capabilities in Singapore and reinforces our commitment to the maritime, aviation, and broader transportation sectors across the Asia-Pacific region and beyond. Working closely with our shipping teams in Hong Kong and Shanghai and our aviation team in Singapore as part of our global Transportation Industry Group, Gregory will enhance our ability to provide seamless, cross-border support to clients operating in one of the world's most dynamic industries."

Xu commented: "Reed Smith's global platform, sector focus, and collaborative culture are hugely impressive. The firm's market-leading finance practice and its long-standing reputation in general banking, asset finance, and project finance were major draws for me. I'm delighted to be joining such a highly collegiate team with a clear strategy for growth in the region and look forward to contributing to the continued expansion of the firm's finance offering across Asia-Pacific."

Praj Samant, Reed Smith's Asia-Pacific managing partner, said: "We are delighted to welcome Gregory to the Singapore team. His arrival further strengthens our offering in one of the region's most strategically important sectors. We look forward to the valuable contributions he will bring to the firm locally and globally."

Xu is recognized as a leading practitioner by multiple legal directories. Chambers Asia-Pacific ranks him as a "Leading Individual" for Banking & Finance (India), while The Legal 500 identifies him as a "Leading Partner," "Next Generation Partner," and "Recommended" lawyer across Banking & Finance, Asset Finance, Projects, and Shipping in Singapore and in the foreign juris

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SIDLEY AUSTIN LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ SIDLEY ADVISES ON BROOKFIELD INDIA REIT AND POONAWALLA FINCORP QUALIFIED INSTITUTIONS PLACEMENTS

Sidley has advised on two significant qualified institutions placements (QIPs) in India, for Brookfield India Real Estate Trust (Brookfield India REIT) and Poonawalla Fincorp, together raising over ₹5,100 crore (US$550 million).

Source: Company Website

+ SIDLEY REPRESENTS APOLLO ON ITS STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN VVARDIS

Sidley is representing funds managed by Apollo ("Apollo Funds") on their strategic minority investment in vVARDIS Holding AG ("vVARDIS"), a high-growth Swiss dental medtech company focused on non-invasive treatment solutions.

Source: Company Website

+ TEN SIDLEY LAWYERS NAMED AMONG D MAGAZINE'S 2026 "BEST LAWYERS IN DALLAS"

Share D Magazine has named 10 Sidley lawyers to its annual "Best Lawyers in Dallas" list. Lawyers are nominated by their peers, then reviewed by an anonymous panel of lawyers and D Magazine's editorial staff. Recognized Sidley lawyers are:

Sara Garcia Duran Kelly M. Dybala Yolanda C. Garcia Lauren G. Grau William D. Howell Tara M. Lancaster S. Scott Parel Rakhee V. Patel Eric M. Winwood Angela C. Zambrano
